An open letter to the girl from engineering I don’t know if this letter will ever reach you in the way I intend, but I needed to write it anyway. There are things I should have said before, and things I wish I had understood sooner. Most of all, I’m sorry. Sorry for the ways I hurt you whether I realized it at the time or not. Sorry for the moments I chose silence when I should have spoken, distance when I should have stayed, and pride when I should have been gentle. I see now how my actions, and sometimes my inaction, created wounds that didn’t deserve to be there. Regret has a quiet way of staying with you. It shows up in the small hours, in the memories that replay differently when you finally understand what they meant. I’ve had time to reflect, and in that reflection, I see more clearly the weight of what I lost—not just the relationship, but the trust, the warmth, and the version of us that could have been. You deserved better from me. That truth sits heavily, but it’s one I accept fully. I’m not writing this to reopen anything or to ask for another chance. Some things, once broken, aren’t meant to be rebuilt and I respect that. I’m writing because you deserved an apology that didn’t come too late or too quietly. I hope you’ve found peace. The kind that isn’t interrupted by old memories or unanswered questions. I hope your days are lighter now, filled with people and moments that treat your heart with the care it always deserved. And if my absence has brought you that peace, then I accept that too. You mattered to me more than I showed, more than I knew how to express at the time. That won’t change, even as life moves us further apart. Take care of yourself. Truly.

I don’t usually engage in this type of thing, but I think this has already gone too far. Yes, we were given equal days to study and prepare for the exam, but if the situation were reversed, don’t you think you would also question why others got a more manageable exam? We weren’t even questioning the type of questions or the format, but rather the coverage, because we spent time and effort attending makeup classes just to cover all the topics indicated in the Table of Specification (TOS). We are not questioning the difficulty of the exam you were given, but the system and what led to the creation of a different set, when since first year we’ve been taking departmental exams. So maybe shut your privileged ass and just be thankful you got a different set of the exam 🙂

Completely unrelated to anything just my realization to how I’ve been living my life lately: I recently talked to someone (after years of not being in a relationship), and after some time, they decided they weren’t ready to start anything yet. So we ended it. We hardly even knew each other within those times, so, supposedly it should’ve been easy to get over it right? Well, I guess not for me. Perhaps I got used to the routine of talking to them, so I was so desperate to have them back. In my desperation, I sent them a message (begging them to continue talking like before even without the expectation of dating). They turned me down [REDACTED_NAME]. They told me that wouldn’t be fair to either of us. I get blocked and I completely lose myself. After a couple days, I downloaded a dating app and matched with someone. They were overtly sexual, constantly asking me to show them my body, touch my body, and tells me stories about their past sexual experiences. I’ve never had sex before, and the conversation of sex makes me uncomfortable. But I liked the attention. I liked that they kept asking for me, it made me feel like I was finally wanted. One night, we were talking (we are not in the same time zone so practically it was already late but for them it was only 5pm), they told me they wanted to see me, I say its already late and wanted to sleep. They get mad, since I’m not doing what they were asking. I told them I don’t want to talk to them anymore if they’re going to treat me that way, and they cussed me out. I was then blocked the morning of that conversation. Again, in desperation, I still reached out. Telling them I’m sorry, that I was just tired, and I’ll make it up to them. I was blocked for the third time. I kept telling myself I was smart. I told myself that I wouldn’t bring myself that low just to feel loved. In the end, I realized I did bring myself that low. I let someone sexualize me, just to feel like I was wanted. I offered myself to be used for conversation, so I don’t lose someone. In my desperation to feel loved, I started to lose my self respect. How disgusting it is to find myself in this situation.

Ang hirap lumaban ng patas sa [CAR]. Instead of fostering fairness, some policies appear to result in shared consequences where even those uninvolved in wrongdoing are affected. In such cases, students who act with integrity are still affected in the same way, raising serious concerns about fairness. This reflects a deeper issue that continues to weaken students’ trust in the system. When students raise valid concerns, these are often dismissed or turned back on them. Those who speak out are met with further difficulty. Assessments become more challenging, and the academic environment grows more hostile than responsive. Now, concerns are also being raised regarding the fairness of examinations themselves. Questions persist as to whether uniform standards were upheld and whether all students were assesses under equivalent conditions. If the measures used to evaluate learning are themselves called into question, it becomes difficult to expect students to maintain trust in the system. This is not simply a complaint, but a call to uphold the standards and values the institution claims to stand for.

Sometimes in college, you meet a lot of people in your first year and it feels like you’ve found your circle. But as time passes, you realize it slowly gets smaller. Not all friendships are real or meant to last. Some people stay only when they need something, and disappear when they don’t. I know everyone has their own priorities and busy lives. People change, relationships shift, and sometimes distance just happens. So choose your circle wisely. Set boundaries. Protect your peace. Not everyone deserves full access to your life. It’s okay to go back to people who have truly been there for you, even from before college. “Consistency reveals real friends.” “Some people are only temporary chapters.” “Protect your peace, not just your presence.”

Message to Teachers on Online Classes (SONAHBS) Online classes are meant to help us adjust, but they're starting to feel overwhelming. I’m writing this in hopes that we can make better use of the time in this setup. Instead of adding extra activities that wouldn’t normally be given in a face-to-face class, it might be more effective if we were given this time for actual studying (through discussions or asynchronous reading assignments ). I understand that our educational system in the Philippines is very output-based, but we already have a heavy enough workload even in the regular full face-to-face setup. Adding more tasks on top of that (during the OLC) leaves little time to actually understand and review the material (speaking as a frustrated learner who needs more time to study). At times, it feels like the online period is not being used as intentionally as it could be, and it ends up becoming counterproductive. I also know that this shift has been sudden and challenging for teachers as well. But at the same time, it’s frustrating when time and effort are spent on activities that don’t meaningfully contribute to learning. Not all courses benefit from more written outputs. Many of our subjects require us to spend long periods studying, understanding, and memorizing the material, and we need time for that. This is not for everyone, as some instructors have been able to use the time well, and put real effort into teaching the material. I really appreciate that. I just hope this reaches the right audience who may actually need to rethink their approach. We are all in a difficult situation, and we hope that our teachers can be more careful in deciding what will truly help the students, rather than simply assigning tasks for the sake of having something to fill in the time.

Adults in Age, Not in Conflict You’re an adult. You witness a colleague handle a concern the right way—privately, directly, and with the appropriate people involved. Then, unexpectedly, someone else chooses a different route. Instead of addressing the matter where it belongs, they involve a “protector” and shift the conversation into something public, indirect, and quietly hostile. You start to wonder—what exactly is this behavior? If you’ve experienced this, take note: your instinct isn’t misplaced. What you’re seeing isn’t really about resolution. It’s a pattern. At its core, it often reflects a conflict style built on avoidance and image protection. Some individuals struggle with direct, uncomfortable conversations. Rather than engaging head-on, they navigate conflict in ways that preserve how they are perceived, even if it complicates the situation. What tends to drive this? Emotional immaturity. Not everyone develops the same level of emotional regulation, accountability, or direct problem-solving. Age alone doesn’t guarantee growth—intentional effort does. Avoidance of confrontation. Discomfort with being challenged, uncertainty in expressing their side, or fear of vulnerability can lead people to take indirect routes instead of addressing issues directly. Image management. Maintaining the appearance of being “right” or “wronged” can become more important than resolving the issue itself. So the narrative gets shaped early—often before the full story is even heard. Power over communication. Bringing in a third party shifts the dynamic. Instead of a balanced conversation, it becomes a situation of leverage. This is less about dialogue and more about advantage. Learned patterns. For some, this isn’t new behavior. It may stem from environments where indirect handling of conflict was normalized—or even rewarded. Indirect expression. Frustrations don’t disappear; they simply surface in subtler ways—side comments, selective storytelling, or passive-aggressive remarks. In essence: Avoidance + insecurity + narrative control = indirect conflict. And that’s the key point—this isn’t someone seeking resolution. It’s someone seeking comfort, validation, or control. So when a calm, straightforward conversation doesn’t work, it’s not necessarily a failure on your part. It’s a mismatch in approach. To wrap things up—karma has a way of arriving right on time. I only wish I had front-row seats when it does. Sending this off with nothing but class and composure.

<3 Hey there, I just wanted to say I am proud of you! To everyone who chose to show up, to those who tries to be present, to those who—no matter how cruel the world can be—still choose kindness and compassion, still choose to live for another day, still puts an effort for yourselves even when you feel like you’re going no where...The fact that you are still here and living is progress in and of itself. You don’t need perfect grades, constant preparedness for exams, a big circle of friends, unecessary luxury things, or a flawless appearance. You don’t need to overextend yourself, prove your worth, or pretend you’re okay all the time. You don’t have to earn rest, love, or belonging. You are already enough—worthy, whole, and deserving, exactly as you are, even on your quiet, uncertain days. The love you are bound to receive is not measured by your worldy successes or what you bring to the table. This is the universe reminding you that “It’s okay, not to be okay.” Feel the disappointment, feel the pain, feel the anger, feel the failures—But don’t forget to pick yourself up because no one else will. You’ve got this!

Adding to the concern already raised regarding the hard hatting ceremony The guidelines initially stated that only students who are eligible -those who have taken or are will be taking their OJT next short term, are allowed to attend. However, during the HyFlex meeting, it was mentioned that there are additional slots for those planning to take their OJT next regular semester. What is difficult to understand is why some PICE officers and their friends who are not originally eligible were accommodated first. If additional slots were available, why were these not opened and fairly offered to all students from the beginning? This creates a clear issue of fairness and transparency. Opportunities like this should not appear selective or prioritized for a specific group, especially when guidelines were already set. I also hope that decisions like this are made without any bias from the faculty and are based solely on fair and consistent criteria. A clear explanation is needed on how participants were chosen and why this process was not communicated earlier. This situation is unfair to those who followed the given criteria and were not given the same chance.

"I'm stuck here but I might ask for the bill sooner than I expected." I've been in this restaurant before. It was beautiful and full of warmth. The first time, it was very nice. Not the greatest menu but I was content with everything. I mean, I was being served well. But that was just in the beginning. The menu became stale as time went by, and the bountiful feast turned into crumbs. But I was down deep already that I still found myself settling for those crumbs. Because I thought, maybe this restaurant just lacked the ingredients for now. But I was wrong. It went on for long that I just decided to leave. But of course, I regretted it. Months later, the restaurant welcomed me again. On the outside, it seemed to have been renovated. I was served better and the menu was great. I was happy again and thought, maybe this time is really 'the moment'. But it was really just that - a moment, for it didn't last long. Same old thing happened again. The restaurant just couldn't keep up with what it used to be at the beginning. I found myself settling for crumbs again. This time, however, was more painful. I chose to stay in this restaurant because I had hoped that maybe it just needed some time to grow. But in the middle of waiting, the lights went out. I knew everything was not right already but to completely shut off without warning - I... I'm just left here wondering what I was to you after all this time. What my purpose or my place here was. Because I truly believed that I belonged here. You made me. I tried calling for your name but to no avail, my voice just echoed through the desolate corners of this restaurant. In this darkness, I was left here to wait alone. I'm just tired of it all. Because even after all the things you did, I still chose you. But you couldn't even bring yourself to face me and tell me the very thing I just needed to hear from you. You turn avoidant when things get serious. And then you try to pull back whenever I took a step back. It's a cycle and I'm always at the losing end. I'm stuck here like a ghost that haunts your walls but I may choose to leave for good now. Maybe one of these days, I'll finally ask for the bill. But I'll just stay a little longer. I miss you but I miss myself more now. If we are really meant for one another, maybe we'll find each other again - when you can finally meet me in between. - 🎨

I am an autistic person with [REDACTED_NAME], and i wrote a 93k novel in just 2 weeks, and this series is already out. I can't tell you the title, but it's out there. Plot Twist: I am an autistic [CAR] Alumnus, [REDACTED_NAME], CSC Passer, who denied entry in [REDACTED_NAME], but has relatives in [REDACTED_NAME], and relatives in psychiatry... I am an advocate, and people call me insane for it. That's all you need to know. Feel free to learn more. For those who knew me, you will know who I am. And I don't give a damn about it. "Yes. I moved past from it. In truth, what happened was I stood up for what I perceived was injustice and place it all over the one I was in love with who kept placing mixed signals with me. I... stood up to them... sent a message to the class gc standing up for myself... I said the wrong things... Cuz I panicked Cuz they sent a picture to that group chat... where they went to a party after making sure I won't join... A repetitive pattern. I told the whole class my side because I guess I was emotional after they sent a picture in the whole class of them in a party while I wasn't there. I entered a dark place and people just outcasted me after she said her side, and treated me like an autistic menace. Admittedly, both sides were emotional, but I shoudln't have spoken against anything the said. I wrote the second book, [REDACTED_NAME], as an apology, and put myself in my enemy's shoes of how he views me... Thus in lore it was written by [REDACTED_NAME] in-universe. I told them the story was a cry for help, and at the time, I expessed to essentially off myself and they didn't care, saying "We are not obligated for your feelings." The ultimate gaslighter's word to flip me as the enemy, and flip my life as nothing but fodder. Horrible people. Last month, I was cyber attacked by who I thought was an ally, but I learned their little harassment secret through dediuction alone, and they didn't deny it, so I blocked them. These people are nothing but spineless if they still hold onto what I did, what they did, and not doing anything about it instead just walk around in their room thinking of a plan to destroy me. Because that lady looked at every one of my stories in a certain social media posting website, and they accused me of placing me into that group, which was funny because that was a character who wasn't supposed to even be there. Similar origin, but not them. I'll let you all figure out who it is. I didn't decide to adapt that version of the story, lol. But anyway, this whole thing was to show a more positive way and outlook for my OC, which was why the other middle child books, were given mixed reactions. Because it sucked.., Anyway, the person last month kept mocking my feelings and the fact I couldn't move on, so I blocked them and laughed at them because they were the one who couldn't move on. Another context is my family wanted legal action, and I was gonna join the bandwagon, but [REDACTED_NAME] stopped me and told me I'd be ruining this poor kid's future. I cried, and realized what was the right thing to do, that this [REDACTED_NAME], REGARDLESS of how disgusting he acted, deserved kindness. So... I knew what to do To bite my tongue and let graduation roll out and then confront them. They honestly couldn't do anything, neither could I. Why? Well, one of em cyberattacked me online when I adverted my book. Had a panic attack, a heart attack, and people keep downplaying how fucking traumatizing it was for me. My family intervened and wanted legal action on this person, and my mother took over of my account to block him. The party legitimately thought I lied... What a disgusting monster. The worst part of society, is that they always destroy the reaction, rather than thinking about what provoked such. That, by definition, is shallow, and a lack of self awareness. They said I lacked self awareness for an apology, for a book. A book that destroyed your ego... Bleed it, my friend... Bleed your ego, and let Christ into your heart, or whoever you believe in. If not, you are not Batman. If you did, I am very, very proud of you, [REDACTED_NAME]. I spoke, and that itself is a reflection. You're silent, clever, powerful, and that is an even larger mirror towards you. Imagine making a little hater's club just to monitor me... That is ultimately pathetic. YES, I wrote a novel, because I myself am pathetic, for getting traumatized by a narcissistic monster like you, (let's call him after the lady from Fairly Odd Parents" [REDACTED_NAME]. If you honestly think you are the hero like my Mom said? You are a very dangerously intelligent man. Mommy still wants dialogue you denied. I'm the only thing keeping your narration, your story, your ego alive. I was "too emotional" and "I cared too much". I should've "manned up". I "lacked self-awareness". I also "was a troubled person". They "felt sorry for me". Society is... a superficially shallow one. Especially Philippine Society as a whole. I have seen people judge you for what you are and call you out for being wrong because of its "conservative" ways. What is tradition/Conservatism? Tradition is essentially the return to your roots, the practice and the art of your culture. It should be about the belief in where you belong. Instead, grifters twist it into something much, much stupider. Apparently we are all rooted in toxicity and gatekeeping to them. Apparently, conservatism means us vs. them. Liberalism is another thing. It's supposed to mean progress. Instead, Liberals abuse it to gain more rights. I, myself, may have been guilty of this in the past. And I argue that this isn't fair to the idea of equity. True equity is achieved when each person respects each other.. regardless... Liberals and Conservatives are BOTH NEEDED. Liberals progress. Conservatives stagnate the movement. Liberals, you are our engine. Be mighty. Conservatives, you are our brakes. Level. They called me to man up because I gave a damn. But look at our history. People who give a damn always, always wrote. Always wrote to become better, stronger, greater... And build things to the world we know today. Palaces, cathedrals, books, stories, and here we are... I don't wanna forgive myself, but I have to, because I am just as [REDACTED_NAME] as you all are. I... ended up making a friend, my romantic interest cry after a panic attack... The guy, [REDACTED_NAME], twisted my words into harassment. Absolutely genius. They all did, when I tried to say my side. What's disgusting is when I shared with him memes, he'd play along, and now he's saying that I harassed him by pretending to be bi. I said my crush on him was A JOKE, and the lady I blocked BELIEVED him... He was a genius, and made sure every word I shared, every thing I shared about my trauma since I thought they were my friends, were taken against me due to my oversharing, due to the fact I stood up for myself... Theys ent a picture of them, [REDACTED_NAME] did, sent a picture of them hanging around while I was told to stay at home... They kept looking for excuses, that I'd panic if I were there, that CRUSH wasn't even there, but I yelled at CRUSH through text because she hurt me, invalidated my existence, and with cynicism, repeatedly never believed in me. What hurt the most is when she said [REDACTED_NAME] has diskarte, and I did not. It invalidated my strengths, my personhood, and my sacrifices. [REDACTED_NAME] was just too good, and I almost felt envious toward him. But that envy turned to pity, when he used every joke, every trauma, and called me a psychopath. "DARVO" (Deny, Attack, and [REDACTED_NAME] and Offender) It is very humorous, that you twisted reality into your own mouth... It doesn't matter if you sue me for defamation, libel... whatever... All I give a damn about is I tell the world the truth, fix the world, and abandon this conflict. I forgive, but I will never let us forget." Go on. Mock me. Huddle in a circle and mock me as we once did when we shared a table together. Go on, make inside jokes, laugh at me. It is further proof of your moral corruption. But I will never, ever change, conform to this horrible, unfair world, for I am autistic. That is what autism is. I don't care if you enter law school to prove power over me. I don't care if my reputation will be destroyed for telling the truth. I don't care if you destroy me and hate me. Because you will always be... my friends. I'm sorry I loved that fair lady and it came off obsessive or forceful... Autistic persons love, and they get too attached, we don't know how to make social boundaries, and stay before the lines. No, I didn't do anything illegal. But if I did, when I stand before God, may I be struck down, for that is the right thing to do. Am I oversharing? YES... Because this is justice and authenticity. I will NOT apologize for being unable to understand social cues, for being labeled insane, for being labeled as someone who doesn't understand social cues, social boundaries, social thingies that I can't see or feel or understand because whenever I act different I get judged for it, for being infantilized by my people. But I will apologize for whatever you blame me for. I will never ask for forgiveness, because I apologize not for your ego, but for my heart. And to those who know him, please don't hunt [REDACTED_NAME] down. He is my friend. I had... a marvelous journey writing this story... See y'all soon. Feel free to reveal who I am... I'd be very, very excited if someone is dumb enough to do so. And actually, I'm counting on it. The chapter releases soon, you wicked monsters.

Bridging Generations. . . We stand today at the meeting point of two worlds—the world that shaped us, and the world shaping the youth. Our generation grew up in libraries, turning pages with patience, searching for wisdom line by line. The younger generation grows up with the internet, where answers appear in seconds, and life moves at lightning speed. It is easy to compare, but comparison is not the path forward. The truth is, they are not weaker for living differently. They are stronger for adapting to the world they were given. Their resilience lies in coping with rapid change, just as ours lay in resourcefulness and persistence. Our role as the older generation is not to judge, but to guide. To meet them halfway. To listen, to understand, and to pass on the values that never fade—respect, kindness, perseverance, accountability and responsibility. These are the bridges that connect us, no matter how wide the gap may seem. Generations may differ in tools, but not in humanity. The internet may replace the library, but it cannot replace the values that make us whole. Let us stop measuring the youth against the past, and instead embrace them as the future. Together, let us build a world where wisdom and innovation walk side by side. Where the strength of yesterday meets our youth today. Let’s hear them. Let’s understand them. Let’s learn from them.

A messy rant from someone who's tired af but keeps going for myself: It's true na maraming resources ngayon at easily accessible because of the internet, mabilis na google search lng may sagot [REDACTED_NAME], mas lalo na at may AI na rin (altho I hate generative AI and I refuse to use it, I'll submit a low quality essay than use AI, that's MY principle so no offense). But if we reallyyy think about it, are these resources even factual? Also, many are behind pay walls and for ME I don't want to voluntarily give ANY personal information on an online website even if it's secured, plus following the syllabus, if the prof even gives a syllabus, isn't even guarantee coz the references listed are sometimes not easily accessible genuinely. And it's so easy to spread misinformation because of how easily accessible almost everything is. Just because there's many resources doesn't mean it's useful. Some topics are HARD to find. This is coming from someone who lost sleeping searching all over the internet finding references that are factual, peer reviewed, and up to date (so many papers r so old that it doesn't fit the given criteria). And WHAT resources are we gonna use? The time dedicated to other equally/more important things is gonna be eaten up by searching the materials needed. So stop using "there's so many resources nowadays" as an excuse to INVALIDATE the students who are SPEAKING UP coz YOU COULDN'T. We're the ones who has to fix the old gens negligence just so we could LIVE in a world where you don't have to be in constant survival mode, the least you can do is be open minded about change, did history not teach you that society is dynamic? ([REDACTED_NAME] nilalahat, pero kung sino pa sarado ang utak sila pa malakas mag ingay) "[REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_NAME] noon..." I'm not asking for your suffering, I'm asking for support, I'm not a therapist. Just coz you had a hard time doesn't mean we also have to suffer for you to feel good abt yourself. But isn't that the point? To have the young gens lesser insignificant hardships coz 'bata ang pag-asa ng bayan'? (I hate that phrase, I get the thought but now it's just giving us problems to solve that shouldn't even be a problem in the first place if ppl matured) I'm not angry, I'm just disappointed that the generations that should've matured and teach wisdom are the ones who are against us. So no, we don't have it easy, nor the next generations will have it easy, times change, keep up and break the cycle. Think about it, if Filipino children could've chosen their passion, wouldn't we develop into a better country? They would actually enjoy learning and give back to the community that gave them that opportunity and support. "The real world ain't like that" The real world is that we can CHANGE, the real world before were wars, salvery, colonizations, and so much worse but thankfully people SPOKE UP, the revolution didn't suddenly start but it started small. I'm not saying we need a bloody revolution, I'm just saying that even if it's different today, there's still injustice that we can clearly see so we give attention to it. And why tf are you bashing someone younger than you? Please reflect on yourself, genuinely. There's so much more to talk abt but then I'd reach the character limit. TL;DR: Your suffering from before doesn't give you the right to invalidate others who's in a different time and place than you.

Somehow I struggle to remember any achievements I've made in my life. I can't remember anything off the top of my head, I have to struggle to remember even one. And even when I do, it feels pointless. Meaningless. Like none of it will ever be enough. Because I wasn't doing it for myself, was I? I was doing it for people who wanted to see me do it. It was all I ever heard. Every diploma. Every medal. Every fancy piece of paper that said I was the best. I hoped that they would make me worth loving, because that was the only way I knew how to earn that. Yet every mishap between or after only reminded me that I am worth less than the dirt I tread upon. I am not worth loving if I am not performing at my best. I am not worth sparing an afterthought. I am nothing more than a statistic. A report card. A dusty medal hanging from a picture frame. If any of that is even worth remembering.

Let’s hear it from an Alumni. I have been with [CAR] since birth until I graduated from college, and now my child is also with [CAR], taking up a pre-med course. During RetDem days, professors gave clear instructions—guidelines, dos and don’ts were emphasized. Yet, there was one incident where a student failed to properly dispose materials used in their lab activity. The prof asked the class to identify who was responsible, but no one admitted to the mistake. As a result, the prof announced that the entire class would receive a deduction unless the accountable student spoke up. This situation created anxiety among students. They worked hard to earn decent scores, but because of one person’s mistake, everyone’s efforts were penalized. While accountability is indeed a vital value—especially for future professionals who will handle lives—it should be taught in ways that do not unfairly punish others. For example, accountability could be reinforced through practical measures such as: Providing designated trash bins for each group or Using color-coded materials to trace responsibility more easily. In the real world, mistakes are handled through due process. Investigations are conducted before sanctions are given, ensuring that the right person is held accountable. In this case, the sanction relied on the hope that the violator would admit the mistake, but when that doesn’t happen, the entire class suffers. I respectfully urge the institution to adopt fairer and more just approaches in handling such situations. [CAR] has been an esteemed institution for decades, and surely there are established guidelines for addressing these scenarios. Please protect the integrity of the students’ hard work—they should not have to bear the consequences of another’s defiance.

An Open Letter to the Administrators of the [CAR] Freedom Wall To the Administrators, I am writing to express a growing concern regarding the nature of some entries being posted on the [CAR] Freedom Wall. While it is widely understood that a freedom wall serves as a platform for expression, it is equally important to recognize that freedom of speech should not come at the expense of respect and dignity. It has become noticeable that certain posts go beyond harmless expression and instead reflect elements of bullying, personal attacks and even body shaming. These types of entries can harm individuals emotionally and create a toxic environment within the community. As moderators, you hold the responsibility of ensuring that the platform remains a safe space for all students. Freedom of expression should always be balanced with accountability. Allowing posts that target individuals, spread negativity, or encourage hostility contradicts the very essence of a supportive academic community. It is not about restricting voices, but about guiding them toward respectful and constructive communication. I respectfully urge you to strengthen your screening process and be more mindful of the content being approved for posting. Setting clearer boundaries against bullying and personal attacks will help preserve the integrity of the platform and protect the well-being of its audience. The [CAR] Freedom Wall has the potential to be a space for connection, support, and meaningful expression. I hope that moving forward, it can better reflect the values of respect, empathy, and responsibility that the [CAR] community stands for. pangalawang entry [REDACTED_NAME] to bat ayaw niyong ipost to

regarding the 3rd hard hatting ceremony, it has been clearly communicated that only students who meet the following criteria are eligible to participate: (1) completion of 153 academic units, and (2) currently taking or having completed the course principles of steel design. as this ceremony is intended to precede ojt, these requirements are expected to be applied fairly and consistently to all. however, it has come to attention that certain pice officers and their friends who have not met either of these requirements, are still being allowed to participate. it was even mentioned among the officers themselves that these individuals would be “palulusutin” in order to join the ceremony. this raises a valid concern regarding the purpose of requiring other students to submit their curriculum checklists and verify their eligibility, if exceptions are being selectively granted. such actions create a sense of unfairness, particularly for third-year students who were unable to participate due to incomplete units or not yet taking the required course. the same concern also extends to batch 223 students who were not able to join the previous ceremony under similar conditions. while leadership positions carry responsibility, they should also uphold fairness and integrity, especially in matters that affect the student body as a whole. i respectfully urge the faculty in charge to review this situation and ensure that the guidelines are implemented equitably, given that the officers responsible for enforcing these requirements appear to be making exceptions for their peers. this concern is not raised to create conflict, but to advocate for transparency, fairness, and accountability within our academic community.

It was a sunny afternoon, [REDACTED_NAME] 9th. I had just finished our departmental exam, thrilled to go home and reunite with the warmth and comfort of my bed. I was on my way home, riding a jeep. It was hot and humid inside; the feeling can almost compare to a sardine trapped in a small, tin can. The atmosphere felt heavy. Everyone has their own battles to face, responsibilities to fulfill and goals to achieve. As I looked around, my gaze pinned onto the rearview mirror—giving me the opportunity to observe manong driver. His tired eyes were filled with great sorrow. He let out a mild sigh every once in a while as he maneuvered the steering wheel. He knew every press of the accelerator counted. The wheel spinning was an indication that the engine was consuming amounts of fuel that costs a limb in this currently declining economy. Despite knowing the mechanism of how the jeep operated, manong also knew it was getting harder and harder to survive. With increasing inflation and gas prices—and the economy being at all-time low—a job that does not meet minimum wage yet requires intensive labor obviously feels pointless to endure. It’s heartbreaking to see people suffering while the government acts as if we aren’t facing a major economic crisis. I wish I could do more. I wish I could ease their pain. Next election, vote for a candidate who is capable. [REDACTED_NAME], tangina. [REDACTED_NAME] kayo sa mga pilipino. 🐥

What’s the point of requiring students to attend face-to-face classes if the entire session is spent passively listening to PowerPoint presentations that could just as easily be delivered online? Physical attendance should be reserved for activities that genuinely benefit from in-person interaction—such as laboratory work, discussions, demonstrations, or collaborative exercises. Otherwise, it becomes an inefficient use of time, energy, and resources for students who often have to commute long distances. Instructors should provide a clear agenda ahead of time, especially for laboratory classes, so students can prepare accordingly and understand whether their presence is truly necessary. A more flexible approach—deciding in advance whether a session will be conducted online or on campus—would allow students to manage their schedules better and avoid unnecessary travel. This is particularly important for those who have multiple classes in a day, where one subject requires them to be physically present, only to have the rest conducted online. The result is wasted time in transit, added financial burden for transportation, and increased fatigue, all of which can negatively impact learning. Additionally, not all students have equal access to reliable transportation. There is also the issue of productivity. Students could use that travel time for studying, completing requirements, or even resting to maintain their well-being. If face-to-face sessions are not being maximized for meaningful engagement, then they risk becoming a formality rather than a valuable educational experience.

These social issues really made me think about the impact and relevance of our future professions, the contributions and differences that they can make to our economy an society. It's just heartbreaking to see how our PUV drivers and farmers are struggling to make their ends meet. The domino effect also comes in, we also struggle as student commuters, thus our parents must also work harder to provide for us. In short, we DO NOT receive our taxes' worth. As an accountancy student who studies taxation, I can say that this country is F*CK*D-UP. Why? Because our freaking taxes are HIGH, compared to our neighboring countries. Mannn, our VAT (12%) itself is the highest among the Southeast Asian countries. On top of that, we also have other taxes such as the excise, income, withholding, just to name a FEW. These thoughts made me think about the importance of education as our future professions can really make a huge impact to our economy and society. No matter the course/program we are in, we must realize its weight because someday, our professions will make-up our economy. Should we be pressured? At some point, yes, if we're compassionate enough to feel for those who are struggling (drivers, farmers) that we decide to someday make a difference for them, for us. Our education also holds the anchor on our voting knowledge, that by being educated also equates to being wise voters. May the knowledge we have influence us in voting for the right politicians. Hindi para tumulad sa mga bumoto ng dahil lng sa kasikatan at pagkakakilanlan. There’s this new professor in one of our lab classes who has extremely high expectations for the quality of our essays. What do you mean we have to answer five questions in just 45 minutes, and each answer must include an introduction, body, and conclusion? Each question is worth 16 points, for a total of 80 points. It was originally supposed to be 20 points each, making it 100 points in total, but she reduced it to 80 and thinks she is already being very considerate because of that. She evaluates our essays using a rubric, but it feels unfair that if you get 5 points, that means you only received about 1 point for each criterion in the rubric. That just doesn’t seem reasonable. Many of us got only 5 points or even 3 points per question. Were our answers really that bad to the point that we received the lowest possible score in every criterion? Be realistic. The technical paper requirement, which is only supposed to be a “mini research,” is treated as if it must be extremely grounded in extensive research, just because she is a skilled and experienced researcher. She often mentions how things are done at the PhD level, when in fact she is teaching a first-year class.

Hi, I just wanna vent about the professor who's making Capstone ABSOLUTE hell for some students. :) To begin, when my team was currently getting adjusted to the Capstone project already having plans and a Gantt chart all set up, the professor (will be referred to as Mr.1) starts the semester off by scheduling a face-to-face meeting @ 4:30PM to 5:30PM to talk about what he wants for the project proper. Nothing else. Why did he want face-to-face instead of synchronous, I don't know. Afterwards, he begins to talk about how his previous blocks never failed when he was the advisor, and that he's expecting us to continue that streak, and he proceeds to talk about that for HALF of the the time, and THEN begins talking about the requirements when it's about 10-20 minutes before the time. Not only that, he took half of that time just talking about Chapter 1. The thing is that after that class, we had to go home during a heavy storm @ 6:07PM. If anything, we could've ended at 5:30PM if it weren't for him just telling us about his students' success. Now, next thing I want to complain about: During one of our scheduled meetings for "consultation". I'm using HEAVY QUOTATION MARKS for consultation. When it was time to meet and some of our groupmates couldn't make it punctually, we asked Mr. [REDACTED_NAME] to start the meeting and inform the late members of what we talked about, but then he INSISTED that it's either all eight of us were attending, or there WILL BE NO MEETING. Excuse me, but just because someone in the group is missing does NOT mean that he can just not do the meeting entirely. That's not fair to even those who attended early. Not only that, when the actual meeting DID commence, one of the members was constantly disconnecting due to an internet problem. He tried to explain that he couldn't keep a stable connection but Mr. [REDACTED_NAME] said that he was "making up excuses" and if he doesn't attend the meeting, he's gonna get kicked out. I spoke with the member at the time, and he did confirm that his WiFi was cutting out cause he was in town with his family and couldn't keep a stable connection. If anything, that's not fair to the student if he gets kicked out for having bad WiFi. He then proceed to ask about how the leadership structure works. For context: we agreed that we get to work on our pace and still be able to deliver a quality output when the deadline comes, since we know that we have other academic courses to tend to and this was the most viable option. Mr. [REDACTED_NAME] then got mad at that structure, and demanded that our group leader was more stricter with deadlines, and demanded we do daily reports, which is just absolutely unnecessary. WHY? The team already HAS A RHYTHM that we can get going and he says that it's not viable? I thought teamwork meant that we get to find the most optimal way for everyone to work together, not turn the group into a corporation simulator. AND TO ADD!! Yes, this is an entire disaster in it of itself: when we showed him our paper, Mr. [REDACTED_NAME] complained it was too simple, and I quote: "If I put this into AI, I bet it will be longer." I'M SORRY, BUT WHY WOULD YOU IMPLY THAT WE USE AI? It's a CAPSTONE PROJECT. I dunno if it's because he has some sort of bias towards a certain course, but that's just disrespectful to the team that was working on the paper. I've heard that in another group, he was INSISTING that they use AI in their research paper!! You wouldn't believe the cherry on top of this cake, but when my group's leader asked for any tips or advice on improvement on the paper, Mr. [REDACTED_NAME] REFUSED to give any! Citing his reason, "did you not listen to my discussion during our face-to-face orientation?" We asked what he meant, and he refused to reply at all or even elaborate on what he meant. We listened to his lessons, but all we remember is the LITTLE INFORMATION HE GAVE ABOUT THE REQUIREMENTS AND MORE ABOUT HIM BOASTING HIS PREVIOUS CLASS. I'm sorry, but I cannot work with a professor like that. What kind of professor not only DOWNPLAYS his class's work, but then proceeds to GLAZE AI USAGE for an ACADEMIC PROJECT that's supposed to PUT US STUDENTS TO THE TEST. When the project was proposed, he was suggesting making something more coding focused than an actual product. I'm not mad per se, I'm more confused on HOW he had the audacity to say such. Thank you [REDACTED_NAME] for listening to my vent! :)

Funny how someone can study politics, talk about ethics, justice, and accountability, yet fail the most basic test of human decency. You stood there reciting principles like they meant something, like you actually lived by them. Lakas mo pa makapost about women’s month. But behind all that, you chose to be the third party in your own friend’s relationship. Not a mistake but a choice. A deliberate betrayal of someone who trusted you enough to call you a friend. What hurts more is how easy it seemed for you. You carried on like nothing happened. Smiling, laughing, showing up like you didn’t just break someone in silence. That kind of damage doesn’t just disappear. It stays with people. It changes them. You built this image, this polished lifestyle people admire. But if they only knew what it’s hiding. Because no amount of aesthetics, status, or carefully curated life can cover the truth. You were willing to destroy someone else’s peace just to satisfy yourself. You didn’t just cross a line. You erased it. And the worst part is, you did it to someone who never thought you were capable of that kind of betrayal. No names. No drama. Just reality. Not everything that looks good is good. And not everyone who talks about integrity actually has it.

How do you keep going when your own parents have told you that you’ll never be good enough for them? Because I’ve spent so much time trying to prove myself: working harder, doing better, fixing every flaw they point out, and somehow it’s still never enough. It’s like no matter what I achieve, they’ve already decided who I am in their eyes. And after hearing it so many times, I don’t even know if I’m trying for myself anymore, or just chasing approval I’ll never get. I’m exhausted, and I don’t know how to keep believing in myself when the people who are supposed to believe in me don’t. This is serious to me, and I really hope people understand that this isn’t something I’m saying lightly or as a joke.

Hello, senior & other readers. I’ve always lurked on here, but today I decided to send one in as a reply to the post tagged on this post. I’ve learned of a beautiful quote when I was feeling behind when my peers graduated earlier than me. The quote said that the only competition I have is that of myself. If life was a race, I’d be behind future me, and yesterday’s me would be behind me. That should be the only competition you have. Not you vs. other people. Everyone else has their own race to run. So stay in your lane and focus on surpassing the future version of you. With that being said, I hope you’re doing well now, you are more than the accolades you have. You are so much more. You are a person who has hobbies, and interests. Those things matter too, not just your accomplishments. Focus on what enriches your life. Not just on what other people expects from you. Please please please learn to live for yourself, and please believe that the things you want now, will all be yours in due time. With the right amount of effort, courage, and love from the ones who matter the most (not just romantically), you’ll be just fine. If no one has said it yet, I’m very proud of you for making progress. Take healing one step at a time, and if you revert back to any old habits, please don’t beat yourself up over it. Instead, be the kindest to yourself when you slip up. This is your reminder that you should never abandon yourself if you feel like the world has abandoned you. No matter how harsh the world is right now, never be harsh towards yourself. You don’t deserve that. Stay safe, and don’t forget to be kind to yourself. Sending hugs with consent to everyone, 🐈‍⬛

A CALL FOR RESPONDENTS 🚦Help us design a safer Session Road!🚶‍♂️ Good day! We are a group of 4th Year Industrial Engineering students from [CAR], and we are currently gathering data for our capstone project. 📝 Title of the Study: PEDESTRIAN BEHAVIOR AND SAFETY AT CROSSWALKS: AN EXTENDED THEORY OF PLANNED BEHAVIOR 🔍 Overview of the Study: Pedestrian safety remains a pressing issue in Baguio City due to heavy foot traffic and challenging topography. Our research aims to understand the behavior of the pedestrian based from newly proposed, ergonomically designed pedestrian crossing mockup for Session Road. We want to understand how specific features (like high-visibility signage and tactile indicators) affect a pedestrian's intention to cross safely instead of violating traffic rules like jaywalking. 🎬 What to Expect in the Survey: To give you a clear picture of our interventions, you will be required to watch short video simulations of the proposed crossing before starting the questionnaire. This video will showcase the design under multiple real-world scenarios: sunny, rainy, foggy, and at night. Once you have visualized the design, you can proceed to answer the questions! We are looking for respondents who regularly navigate and cross Session Road. If that’s you, we would greatly appreciate your input! ⏱️ Estimated Time: It will only take 7 minutes to complete the videos and the survey! 🔗 Link to the Questionnaire: 🛡️ Data Privacy Assurance: Your privacy is our priority. In compliance with the Data Privacy Act of (RA ), all personal information and responses collected will be treated with the utmost confidentiality and will be used exclusively for the purpose of this academic research. Thank you so much for your time, effort, and support in helping us build a safer, more pedestrian-friendly Baguio City! Please feel free to share this post with your friends and family. Stay safe! ✨

I’ll just rant here for a bit. I admit I got interested in this guy last year when we became classmates. At first, I thought it was nothing more than curiosity about his personality. But slowly, it turned into something I couldn’t quite handle—seeing him often, being around him, and feeling something I kept trying to deny. Honestly, I was frustrated with myself. I kept insisting it was just curiosity—not a crush, not anything deeper. But I couldn’t ignore the quiet disappointment when I realized he wasn’t the person I imagined him to be. You know that feeling when the version of someone in your mind doesn’t match who they really are? Yeah... that. As someone who values boundaries—especially with other girls—I kept everything lowkey. Even my friend here at [CAR] didn’t notice a thing; she was genuinely surprised when she found out. I guess that’s my “talent”... knowing how to hide what I feel. But my closest friends back in my province know the truth—I usually keep my distance from guys. They’ve always been a bit of a pet peeve of mine (sorry 😭). Right now, all I want is to finish this semester and quietly walk away—from them, and especially from him. This is the first time I’ve ever felt like I was losing control over my own emotions. Before, I never allowed myself to go this deep into someone’s world... not until I met him. Still, I’m thankful. Somehow, he gave me reasons to step back and see things clearly. He’s caring and reliable, I’ll give him that—but he’s also a people pleaser. Even when he doesn’t genuinely like someone, he still stays, still talks, still blends in. He’s friends with everyone, and maybe that’s not a flaw. Maybe I just expected him to be different—to be something more. Now, I’ve started avoiding him, and I think he’s noticed. But it doesn’t really matter anymore. Maybe this is how it was always supposed to be—distance and clarity. I’ve never had a boy best friend, and I’ve always said I didn’t like guys... which makes this whole thing feel a little ironic. I can’t believe I almost lost myself in something that was never really mine. Thankfully, I was able to pull away. Two more months of passing encounters, and then I’m free. In the end, it was never me vs. him—it was always me vs. me 😂

It would help to clarify what that actually stands for in practice. [REDACTED_NAME] sandigan my ass. - For a group holding major responsibilities (e.g., EXECOM. SONAHBS), the execution has been underwhelming. Expectations were higher given the platform. Commend [REDACTED_NAME], pero [REDACTED_NAME] projects next time eh catering to students needs more pa. - There’s a noticeable tendency to respond defensively to criticism rather than engage with it constructively ironic for a group that claims to represent student voices. - Concerns about internal culture (including alleged conflicts and treatment of individuals) don’t exactly align with the image presented during elections. - Recurring issues like resignations make you wonder if the problem is situational or systemic. RIGHTS - RIGHTS has potential and is generally well-regarded, but it feels like they’re not maximizing their visibility or influence just yet. - There’s a clear opportunity for them to step up, especially in larger platforms like execom or major events. - A stronger, more amplified presence could position them as a more central force in student leadership. - still playing smaller than they could. time na para ibahin ang political [REDACTED_NAME]. Continuation of They only ever spoke through a screen. It started as nothing—just a shared document passed around between students from UC and [CAR] for a joint project no one really cared about. Names were listed, roles assigned, deadlines ignored. Navi didn’t plan to notice one name in particular. Benjie. No profile picture. No introductions. Just edits—clean, precise, almost annoyingly perfect. At first, Navi found it irritating. Every time he left a messy draft, it would be fixed within the hour. Grammar corrected. Ideas tightened. Even his phrasing—changed just enough to make it better. So he did what anyone would do. He started leaving mistakes on purpose. And [REDACTED_NAME] kept fixing them. Days passed like that—silent back-and-forths hidden in tracked changes. Navi would write something slightly off, and [REDACTED_NAME] would adjust it. No comments. No notes. Just quiet understanding. Until one night, Navi left a line untouched. Not part of the project. Just... there. “Do you ever get tired of fixing things that aren’t yours?” He didn’t expect a response. But the next morning, the document had changed. The line was still there. And beneath it— “Only when they keep breaking on purpose.” Navi stared at the screen longer than he should’ve. Then he smiled. So that’s how it was. From then on, the document wasn’t just a project anymore. It became a conversation. Not obvious—never direct—but threaded between paragraphs and bullet points. “You’re from UC?” Navi typed once, hidden between citations. A few minutes later— “Does it matter?” “A little.” “...[CAR]?” “Yeah.” There was a pause. Longer than usual. Then— “Figures.” Navi laughed quietly to himself. They never exchanged socials. Never sent messages outside that document. But somehow, it felt more honest that way. No faces. No voices. Just words—carefully chosen, deliberately placed. Until the night before the deadline. Navi opened the file, expecting the usual. But something was off. The document was... finished. Perfect. Clean. No mistakes left for him to make. No edits left for [REDACTED_NAME] to fix. And at the very bottom— A final line. “This is enough.” Navi frowned. Enough? That wasn’t how this worked. He typed quickly, fingers moving before he could think. “That’s it?” The cursor blinked. Once. Twice. Then— “Yes.” Something in his chest tightened. “We’re not even done talking.” A longer pause this time. So long Navi thought maybe— “We were never supposed to start.” His jaw clenched. “That didn’t stop you.” No response. Navi leaned closer to the screen, like it would make the silence easier to break. “What, you’re just going to disappear after this?” Minutes passed. Then— “It’s better that way.” Navi exhaled sharply, running a hand through his hair. “For who?” No answer. Of course. He stared at the words, at the neat, polished document that suddenly felt... empty. Like something had been carefully erased. “You always do this?” he typed. This time, the reply came slower. “Do what?” “Leave before things mean something.” The cursor blinked again. Longer. Heavier. Then— “You don’t even know me.” Navi’s fingers hovered over the keyboard. Then pressed down. “I know enough.” Another pause. And for a second—just a second—Navi thought maybe [REDACTED_NAME] would argue. Deny it. Stay. But instead— “Goodbye, Navi.” The name hit harder than it should’ve. Because Navi never told him. Not directly. Not anywhere obvious. Yet somehow— He knew. Navi’s throat felt dry. “Wait—” But the cursor stopped blinking. The document went still. No more edits. No more replies. Just a finished project... and a conversation that ended too cleanly to feel real. Navi stared at the screen until it dimmed. “...You’re seriously just going to leave it like that?” he muttered. But there was no one left to answer. Only that last word sitting quietly at the bottom— Goodbye. And somehow, that felt like the only thing [REDACTED_NAME] ever fixed that Navi couldn’t undo. Ps. To my baby RadTech, Happy birthday. I know you still read posts here. You used to send me things from here all the time. It’s strange how something so small can still remind me of you so easily. It’s been a long time since we last spoke. When I unblocked you on Facebook, you noticed right away. You told me to block you again because you weren’t used to being the one who blocks. That stayed with me more than it should have. I’m taking BS Psychology now in Pampanga. You probably don’t know that. You probably still hate Pampanga, too. There are a lot of things about my life now that you don’t know anymore. I don’t even have any way to reach you since you changed your number. I miss you. More than I have any right to. What makes it worse is that I understand exactly why I lost you. I was the one who broke what we had. I cheated. I took advantage of how much you loved me. I saw you fall apart. I saw how I hurt you, how I crushed something so genuine. And even then, you still chose to give me another chance. I didn’t deserve that. I ruined it again. You didn’t deserve any of it. I tell myself you’ve already moved on. I hope you have. I hope someone is giving you the kind of love I failed to give. The kind that’s patient, honest, and steady. The kind you always deserved. Still, there are nights when I can’t help but think of you. I carry the weight of what I did, and the silence you left behind. I miss you. I always will. If nothing else, I hope I find you in my dreams sometimes.

Navi always noticed him first in crowded places. Not because [REDACTED_NAME] stood out—he didn’t. He blended too well, like someone who learned early that being seen too much meant being asked too many questions. But Navi had a way of catching the small things: the way [REDACTED_NAME] lingered near exits, the way his shoulders tensed whenever someone called out “UC,” the way he never stayed long enough for conversations to settle into something real. They weren’t supposed to meet like this. [CAR] and UC didn’t mix—not in the ways that mattered. Not in the quiet, late-night study halls where exhaustion softened people into honesty. Not in the spaces where rivalry dissolved and something more dangerous could take its place. Yet there [REDACTED_NAME] was, sitting across from him like it wasn’t wrong. “You’re staring again,” [REDACTED_NAME] muttered without looking up. Navi huffed, resting his chin on his hand. “You came here. I think I’m allowed to stare.” “I didn’t come for you.” “Liar.” [REDACTED_NAME]’s pen paused. For a second—just a second—Navi thought he’d look up. Thought their eyes would meet and everything unsaid would spill between them like it always almost did. But [REDACTED_NAME] just kept writing. “You shouldn’t be here,” he said quietly. Navi leaned back, stretching like he didn’t care. Like it didn’t sting. “And you should?” Silence. The kind that pressed. The kind that said more than either of them wanted. Outside, someone laughed—too loud, too bright. It reminded Navi of everything they weren’t. “I heard,” Navi started, voice lighter than he felt, “you’re transferring.” That did it. [REDACTED_NAME] looked up. Finally. And God, Navi hated that his chest tightened at something so simple. “Who told you that?” “So it’s true.” [REDACTED_NAME] exhaled, sharp. “It doesn’t matter.” “It does to me.” The words slipped out before he could stop them. Before he could make them sound like a joke. [REDACTED_NAME] stared at him like he’d just crossed a line neither of them had named. “That’s exactly why it shouldn’t,” [REDACTED_NAME] said, quieter now. “You know how this ends.” Navi laughed, but it cracked. “No, I don’t. Why don’t you tell me?” “You go back to [CAR]. I go back to UC. And whatever this is—” he gestured vaguely between them, like it disgusted him to define it, “—stays here.” “Like it never happened?” “Yes.” The word came too fast. Too rehearsed. Navi swallowed. “You’re good at that.” “At what?” “Pretending things don’t matter.” [REDACTED_NAME]’s jaw tightened. “Not everything has to.” “That’s not true.” “Then what do you want, Navi?” His voice rose just enough to draw a glance from someone nearby before dropping again. “You want me to fight for this? For something that’s already—” “Don’t,” Navi cut in. [REDACTED_NAME] stopped. “Don’t say it like it’s already over,” Navi whispered. Because if he did, then it would be real. And Navi wasn’t ready for real. Not like that. For a moment, [REDACTED_NAME] looked like he might reach across the table. His hand twitched—barely noticeable unless you were looking for it. Navi was. But he didn’t. Of course he didn’t. [REDACTED_NAME] pulled his hand back, curling it into a fist instead. “It’s easier this way.” “For who?” No answer. Navi let out a breath, shaking his head. “You know what I think?” [REDACTED_NAME] didn’t respond, but he didn’t look away either. “I think you’re scared.” A flicker. There, and gone. “I think,” Navi continued, softer now, “you’d rather lose something before it has the chance to matter too much.” “That’s not—” “It is.” Navi smiled, but it hurt. “Because if it matters, then leaving actually hurts, right?” [REDACTED_NAME] stood abruptly, chair scraping against the floor. “We’re done here.” Navi didn’t move. Didn’t stop him. Because this was how it always went—almosts and almosts and almosts until there was nothing left to hold onto. [REDACTED_NAME] hesitated. Just one step away. “Don’t come here again,” he said, not turning back. Navi watched him go. Watched the space he left behind like it might fill itself if he stared long enough. It didn’t. “...You came first,” Navi murmured to the empty chair. And somehow, that made it worse. Because if [REDACTED_NAME] hadn’t come— Then Navi wouldn’t have had anything to lose in the first place. Ps: Na inspire [REDACTED_NAME] ate [REDACTED_NAME] Saturday hehe (peace✌️)

Student Council elections are still a bit far off, but with everything that’s been happening in the council lately, it feels like a good time to reflect on how the different political parties have been performing. This is just personal overall assessment based on observations and experiences, trying to stay as objective as possible. REFORMA-ACS, center-left (left?) - has been around in the council for a long time and clearly has experience and institutional memory. - personal fave: [REDACTED_NAME] term (grounded and more connected to student realities) - They’ve also been involved in negotiations on student concerns (capable of engaging in policy discussions when needed) - recurring concerns that tend to follow the party (instances where their presence in the council felt inconsistent or inactive, especially outside of peak political moments) - During elections, they are often associated with controversies (internal conflicts to allegations of aggressive tactics) which raises questions about their political culture (problematic daw sabi [REDACTED_NAME] from repo na friends) - endorsements and alliances, can also come off as unclear or questionable to some students, making it harder to understand what they firmly stand for. - criticisms about the kinds of candidates they put forward, with some being seen as not fully prepared for the responsibilities of governance. - campaigns are usually polished, strategic, there’s a noticeable gap at times between what is promised and what is actually delivered once in office, which has led to skepticism among some students. 5/10 - hoping [REDACTED_NAME] consistent [REDACTED_NAME] when it comes to what is promised and what is implemented. DASIG, left perspective, ([REDACTED_NAME] aside from puro disruptive protest-good way) - is very visible when it comes to student issues and activism. - show up consistently (pwera nung 1st years [REDACTED_NAME]? [REDACTED_NAME] peak [REDACTED_NAME] heaven ulit and this yr) pero present [REDACTED_NAME] sa rally [REDACTED_NAME] [REDACTED_NAME], in campaigns and in movements related to tuition concerns, dress codes, and even broader national issues. - more militant and advocacy-driven, which works well for students who want a louder and more assertive representation (tho polarizing, especially for those who prefer quieter or more negotiation-based approaches.) - strong presence and engagement with student concerns are hard to ignore. - Their performance can also be seen in the current STELA Assembly, where they appear to be very grounded in terms of representing student sentiments. - evident ang stela assembly. they are frequently talk about them and their initiatives, which suggests that their visibility and connection with students extend across year levels. 100/10 RIGHTScenter-left - seems to take a more balanced and diplomatic approach. - generally good working relationship with the administration, which helps in negotiating and pushing for solutions in a more formal setting - [DEPARTMENT] assembly (wish na ma expand din sila sa main campus with dasig{sana mag collab}) members also tend to have solid leadership backgrounds, which adds to their credibility. -they may not always be as loud or visible in protests or mass actions, but they contribute through dialogue and institutional processes, which can be just as important depending on the situation. 10/10 At the end of the day, it really comes down to what kind of leadership students value more: consistent visibility, strategic negotiation, or a mix of both. Looking at both strengths and weaknesses can hopefully lead to more informed choices in the upcoming elections.

Dear A.A. I am writing to reach you - even if each word I put down is one word further away from the truth that I am so desperately trying to hide from you. I am writing because this is the way I express myself- through thousands of words which I go over and over in my head, thinking if this is the right word for this sentence or if I sound okay or just right or if I’m being too over the top. I am writing this letter because I don’t think I will ever have the courage to break whatever we have now. I am writing this letter because I wanted you to know that I am so grateful to have you in my life, and that I love you. We’ve been friends for over a year. Last semester was when I realized I started having feelings for you. I had no clue how it started, but it just hit me one day when we were at our regular spot in Silang 4th floor, when you brushed my hair away because it was covering my eyes as I was trying to write an assignment for MtLaws. It was awkward, I had to admit - how i felt you stare right at me, but I couldn’t help but feel my heart race. It was then I started to think how much I cared for you. You are one of the people in the world who wants to know the deep and real version of me - the other me I don’t show to our Cof because I’m too scared that they’d get tired of me, which is probably the reason why friend “A” told me that I feel “so near yet so far” during my birthday last November. You take initiative to ask if I’m doing okay, or why I feel down and for that, thank you and I love you. One of my favorite memories this semester was when we went out with our Cof late at night and you accompanied me to the comfort room while holding my hand because you remembered that I was scared of walking alone in the dark due to pass incidents. you didn’t care if people stared, even when i tried to pull my hand away you just grabbed onto it tighter. At one point, I began to think what really mattered to me. That was you, you matter to me. You treated me with kindness, love, and understanding. You made me feel safe and warm and I couldn’t ask for a better friend in the world right now, and maybe it’s alright if that’s what we’ll always be, just bros who are there for each other. I still laugh at the thought of writing a confession letter to you this [REDACTED_NAME] 1st where I poured my heart and soul into saying the words I could not say to your face. And i still remember how you thanked me for loving you and how you said you were sorry for making me feel sad and confused. But you acknowledged what I was feeling and made it clear at that time that we could not be something more. It doesn't change anything, but it does make this a little less painful. I mean how could I have possibly thought that you would see me the same way, you’re a straight guy who is madly in love with our friend and I’m a bisexual guy who is madly in love with you. I am writing this letter because I know you’ll never be able to read this because you currently don’t have a facebook account. I am writing this because I just want to let these feelings out without making you feel burdened of a face to face confession. Maybe, by chance that the feelings I have aren’t really of love but something close to familial love or maybe I’m fooling myself because I don’t want to rob you of your future - how you would always talk about how you want to be married in the Philippines and have your own biological child, because those are things I cannot give you. I promised you that I would be with you forever, and that our friendship would never end no matter what, and that is a promise I am willing to keep. Sincerly, N.D.

An Open Letter to Social Work 3 In all my years of education, I have never experienced an environment as toxic as Social Work 3. Everything feels watched and judged—every movement, every word, even the smallest things. I admit that I am not perfect and that I have also said and done things that were wrong. But this is no longer just about individual mistakes—the environment itself has become the problem. Instead of focusing on learning, it often feels like we are simply trying to survive the pressure and criticism around us. It has reached the point where some of our classmates have already transferred or stopped because of how we treat one another. That alone should make us pause and reflect. Even the professors are, in some ways, part of why this learning environment feels unhealthy. At times, the way concerns are handled, the tone used in addressing students, or the lack of mediation in conflicts can unintentionally add to the pressure rather than ease it. Instead of feeling guided, some of us feel more anxious and hesitant to express ourselves. We are in a Catholic school and are studying Social Work, a field that is built on values like respect, empathy, and compassion. Yet sometimes those values are missing in how we treat each other. If we cannot show basic respect and understanding to our own classmates, how can we expect to serve others in the future? This should be a safe space for us to learn, grow, and support one another—not a place that pushes people away. I hope we all take a moment to reflect. Something needs to change.

To my happy crush from BS Pharma, I’ve learned how to like you from a distance. From across the library floors in Vath. From different programs under the same school. From moments where our eyes almost meet but don’t stay long enough to mean anything. From the quiet space between what I feel and what I dare to show. You probably don’t notice me when I arrive daily at the 4th floor earlier than my classes. I tell myself it’s just to study, just to be productive. But truthfully, it’s also because you’re there. Because somehow, that corner of the library feels different when you are. I sit a few tables away, pretending to read, pretending to focus, but my attention drifts. From the way you tuck your hair behind your ear, to the soft seriousness you carry while working. I’ve memorized the rhythm of your presence. And maybe that’s the hardest part — being close enough to see you, but not close enough to know you. Sometimes I wish I were braver. Brave enough to ask about your day. Brave enough to be more than just another face studying under the same lights. Instead, I stay where I’ve always been...a few steps back, a little out of frame, hoping that one day I won’t just be part of the background of your shift. Until then, I’ll keep showing up a little earlier than I need to. Not just to study — but to be near you, even if you never know why. --🍀--

Long ago, around 25 years ago, [REDACTED_NAME], the Superhero [REDACTED_NAME], an othered individual, defeated [REDACTED_NAME] in the Saint [REDACTED_NAME] Tournament, after which, [REDACTED_NAME] immediately quit as acting Principal. Indeed. Humiliating, berating, causing panic attacks, fainting, and suicides simply with their rigid rules, while also practicing some common abuse. The very abuse that birthed the Superheroes of legend was forged from the bullying of this school. Stress, abuse, and scolding were the most common forms of abuse by these teachers. The teachers were either young and abusive or old and even more abusive, but some were kind, smart, and believed in each student. One teacher started to speak against this abuse. His name was Professor [REDACTED_NAME], an older Orangutanman from Earth-1. He reached this school expecting that the Greater Roman Church practiced love for their community, softening their heart, and loving others. Sadly, oh so sadly, what Professor saw that day was horrible. Students, rigid, quiet, hands on the table, paying strong attention to the man in front of them. Immediately, he gently finished his class, after which, he went to the Principal and complained. Hi, I’m posting anonymously to ask if others have had a similar experience and to ask for advice. I went to the Student Affairs Office to ask about getting excused for upcoming classes due to a family wedding. I didn't know about the school policies on family weddings beforehand. I followed the process, filled out the form, and submitted it properly. I was told that it could not be excused because it was considered a “personal” reason, which I understood and accepted. However, what made the experience difficult was how it was communicated. The staff member raised her voice and immediately said “No,” while crossing her arms. Her posture and facial expression (one eyebrow raised) felt intimidating and judgmental, which honestly caught me off guard. The conversation continued in the same tone even after I already acknowledged the policy. I was also asked questions like “Ano ka ba sa kasal?” and when I answered that I was part of the bridesmaids, it was emphasized repeatedly that weddings are considered personal unless it involves immediate family. This was explained while other people were present in the office, and it felt embarrassing because it was loud enough for nearby tables to hear. To be clear, I am not questioning the policy. I understand that rules exist. I’m more concerned about how the interaction was handled and how overwhelming it felt as a student just trying to clarify proper procedures. After leaving the office, I felt physically unwell and very distressed. I just want to ask: Has anyone else experienced something similar when approaching Student Affairs? Is this the usual way concerns are addressed? What would be the most appropriate and respectful next step for a student in this situation? I’m genuinely looking for shared experiences and advice. Thank you.

SONAHBS students just proved that no matter how tired they are, the best thing they can do is to survive and still show up every single day. Set aside the scores, the rankings, and the pressure to always be the best. What truly matters is the effort, the consistency, and the courage to continue even when everything feels overwhelming. Don’t get me wrong ha every department and program at [CAR] has its own level of difficulty. Each student carries a different kind of load, and no struggle is ever “less” than another. But there’s something deeply admirable about seeing students push through exhaustion, sleepless nights, and endless quizzes, yet still choose to attend class and do their best. I just really want to appreciate my friends who are always there every day, even when may quiz after quiz, laging [REDACTED_NAME], and mentally drained. Seeing them survive makes me wonder, [REDACTED_NAME] nagagawa ‘yon? Maybe it’s discipline, maybe it’s passion, maybe it’s dreams they don’t talk about, or maybe it’s simply the determination to not give up. Whatever it is, it deserves recognition. Their resilience reminds me that success isn’t always about perfect grades but about showing up despite being tired, choosing to continue despite feeling worn out, and believing that all this effort will someday be worth it. To all SONAHBS students and to everyone who keeps going quietly: you’re doing more than enough, and your perseverance matters. 💙
