I guess I have to start this differently now. You left. And I still like you, [REDACTED_NAME]. I guess I'm kind of a creep for holding onto something only I felt. I’m the kind of person who falls easily. I always have been. I fall like it’s instinct, like it’s safer to be in love with the idea of someone than to stand still. And most of the time, I fall out just as easily. It’s a pattern I’ve memorized. A few weeks of intensity, of overthinking every glance, and then I’m back to normal like it never happened. But there have been exceptions. The slow ones. The ones that take their time growing roots. The ones where I don’t realize I’m in too deep until my heart is already betraying me—beating too fast when they’re near, softening at the sound of their name. You were one of those. I tried to tell myself it was just another cycle starting up again. Just me being dramatic. Just a coincidence. But you remembered when I told you how I felt. You asked me about my life like you actually wanted the answers. You did that small, shy wave when we’d see each other. Your bangs falling over your forehead like you didn’t bother to fix them. Your hair in that tight bun, baby hairs at the nape of your neck catching the light. And your laugh—God. That dorky, unfiltered laugh that makes me laugh too, even when I don’t know why. I remember you clearly. The way the air changes when you’re amused. The way my spine straightens and softens at the same time when you say my name. And that’s the problem. You’re gone now, and my world can’t bend around your laughter anymore. It just kept moving. Which feels unfair. You were always out of my league. Whole. Complete. A woman who didn’t need me. And maybe that’s why this hurts the way it does—because I knew, even while I was falling, that I was stepping off something high. Forgive me, for I let it grow anyway. I let myself imagine warmth. Closeness. The sweetness of what could have been, like biting into an apple in the middle of winter and pretending it was summer. I let the roots spread even though I know what happens when I do. I start wanting too much. I start building futures out of glances. And now I’m left holding something that never fully existed. I never even touched you outside my head, so how is it that I still feel like I’ve lost something real? Love is too strong of a word. “Like” is too small. “Admire” feels unfinished. Whatever it was, it lingers. I hoped you wouldn’t vanish. I hoped you wouldn’t turn into something I’d have to remember alone. But here you are—already drifting into that soft, unreachable space in my mind. If you must be a ghost, then at least haunt me gently. Let me keep the way you smiled. The way you said my name. The version of you that existed in those moments. I still like you. I know you don’t feel the same, and I’m not saying this to change that. I just needed you to know. Even in the coldest season Baguio has given me, I still burn for you. Even when you’re not here. Even when there’s nothing left to hold onto. It’s quiet now. No expectations. No hope trying to be bigger than it should be. Just this feeling. -Crtw (submitted this somewhere else just know it's me)

I told my friend I’m not looking for a relationship, and I meant it... but also, I didn’t. The truth is, I do want one. I just don’t think I’m in that place yet—where I actually have the capacity, or maybe the privilege, to really love someone or be a good girlfriend. Why? It’s hard to explain. Part of it is that I can’t give this wonderful girl what I want to give her. I see people who can just... spend easily, plan things, offer stuff without a second thought. And I know, I know, relationships aren’t about that. But for me? I want to feel like I’m providing, like I can meet her needs. I want to feel... needed. And that’s the bad part, I think. Not the wanting to give, but the wanting to be needed. It gets twisted up. It makes my own affection feel like a transaction I’m not qualified to make. So I just... sit with it. I like her so much it feels like a secret I’m keeping from myself. And the biggest secret is that I’m the one telling me no. just a bit of a vent. 🐔

Since there have been many discussions regarding the [CAR] Clinic, I would also like to share my experience. While these are personal observations, I have noticed that similar concerns have been raised by other students as well. I hope these points can be recognized as areas for improvement and serve as a basis for possible amendments in institutional policies and services. I am open to hearing other perspectives as well, as this is meant to encourage constructive discussion. 🙂 📍 1. Inefficient queuing and waiting system The current lining process can be very inconvenient, especially for students with limited vacant hours. Waiting for 1–2 hours for a consultation, and sometimes longer due to additional queues upstairs, can be difficult to manage alongside academic responsibilities. While students are expected to plan ahead, a more efficient appointment or queuing system may help reduce delays and improve accessibility. 📍2. Lack of approachability and patient-centered communication Another concern is the manner in which some staff interact with students upon entering the clinic. The questioning can sometimes feel more interrogative than supportive, which may leave patients feeling anxious or invalidated. Although medical protocols and standards are necessary for proper diagnosis and monitoring, kindness and professionalism in tone and word choice can greatly affect the comfort of students who are already unwell. 📍 3. Difficulty in obtaining medical certificates or assessments Illness is often unpredictable. Students may wake up with symptoms such as fever or dizziness and may initially take medication and rest before seeking consultation. However, some students report being told that they should have visited the clinic while symptoms were at their peak in order to receive proper documentation. This can be challenging, as it may require students to leave their dorms while actively sick and wait in long lines simply to obtain proof of illness. While it is understandable that diagnoses require observable and measurable symptoms, students should still be met with understanding, especially since not everyone has access to private healthcare providers and may rely solely on school services for medical certification. It is also fair to acknowledge the possibility of misuse, but this should be balanced with the reality that many students genuinely seek medical care and support. [CAR] Clinic, I sincerely hope that service delivery can become more efficient, patient-centered, and compassionate, for the welfare of all students. 🙂

I just saw a rant regarding the clinic services, and I want to share my experience as well. I can’t help but notice the huge difference between the [CAR] clinic and the clinic from my high school (which also catered to college students). I had my work immersion at our high school clinic, and one thing I learned there is that they never allow patients to wait for an hour. In fact, staff would get upset if the line took too long because they knew patients were already uncomfortable or unwell. They were also very empathetic and understanding toward whatever condition the patient had. Here in [CAR], the waiting time is honestly exhausting. Even when the line is short, it still takes 1–2 hours. Why are students who are already seeking medical attention made to wait that long? Why is there only one person doing the interview, causing everything to move even slower? And during the interview, you can clearly see from their facial expressions that they seem doubtful of what the student is reporting—as if we’re not being believed. I personally experienced this when I consulted for dysmenorrhea. The doctor initially misunderstood and immediately asked about a medical certificate because I mentioned I needed one. The nurse then explained that a med cert requires a 24-hour observation and asked if I had missed any subjects. I said yes, because that was the reason I needed it. The nurse responded that the consultation should have been before the subject, then added “oh diba” after repeating my condition—said in a way that made it feel like I was lying. What I don’t understand is this policy of needing a consultation before the class when the consultation itself takes so long because of the waiting time. The class I missed was from 7:30–10:30, and given the clinic process, how exactly do they expect students to manage this? Students don’t go to the clinic for fun—we go because we’re in pain, sick, or genuinely need help

Silent Confession ni (M) Kilala mo ba ang pakiramdam? The slow, crushing realization that you've spent years as a ghost, haunting circles you longed to belong to? My life was a performance of people-pleasing, driven by a deep, dark fear: ang takot na mawala ang mga alaala. I became a different person, just to simply belong. I knew my flaws. I was hyper-aware of every single flaw, trying desperately to file them down, but I only ended up spiraling back. [REDACTED_NAME] sinubukan kong [REDACTED_NAME], it felt like I had offended everyone. I had friends, [REDACTED_NAME] [REDACTED_NAME]. I’d change personalities on the fly, exhausted, only to wake up at 3 a.m. sharp with the silence screaming: Okay pa ba sa kanila ang [REDACTED_NAME]? This is the truth of it: sometimes, the happiest person is the loneliest. Their mind is a cage, where they hurt themselves to protect everyone else. [REDACTED_NAME] guys, they leave, knowing their forced happiness will eventually fade. I went through that. I walked away, heavy with guilt. I needed them to tell me what I did wrong. I needed that verdict to change, but there was only silence. That's why I left, I never wanted to blame them even if I speak so ill of them. To me, the memories of what I had mattered kahit nasaktan ako ilang beses. And now, the urge is back. Ang pagnanais na umalis ulit and change every single piece of myself, just so I can finally be part of someone's story (grabe, nagiging side character). But if I did decide to walk away this time, I truly don't think anyone will ask where I went. The only thing they might carry is a sudden, sharp realization when they understand just how much I meant to them, and how much I will always choose their well-being over my own. Ito yung confession [REDACTED_NAME] so if you know me... Please just smile for me and pretend it's normal, I don't want this burden to affect you as well.

Hello! I just wanted to rant a bit and share my experience with the university's handling of certain procedures in hopes of informing others in a similar situation. Keep in mind na this happened a few weeks ago from now. Context: I was looking to transfer from my previous school and incquired at the student affairs office. I was told to provide some requirements (credentials, 2x2, etc.). This was the first time I asked if it was possible for a transferee to retake a previously failed subject. The answer that I was given was, "possible po." So I proceeded to work on gathering my requirements. I'm sure na a lot of my fellow students know na hindi basta basta lang nakukuha [REDACTED_NAME] ganyan na papers. It's a lenghty, timely, and costly process that really takes a toll on you. Especially as a student on a tight budget. Once I had all of the needed requirements, I submitted it and was allowed to fill out my form, take an interview, credit my classes, and await a phone call in order for me to know when I should take the exam and where. Throughout each step, I repeatedly asked if it was possible for a student in my situation to be accepted and retake my failed subject. Each time, I was told that it was possible and I was allowed to proceed to the next step. After a few days I received a call from the office confirming that I would be taking the exam on May 26. We ended the call and only a few hours later I received another call where they asked me to meet them later that afternoon for them to explain something. I go to the SAO the next day and was informed that not only was I not allowed to take the exam but I was also not able to enroll for any other course with a board exam for the very same reason I was constantly worried and asked about. The failed sub. I explained that from the very beginning I've informed them about it and the response I get is "Sorry." [REDACTED_NAME] late lang nila narealize na hindi [REDACTED_NAME]??? ...huh? [REDACTED_NAME] pa ba na umakyat sa dean [REDACTED_NAME] mapansin na may conflict? As a professional. A guide. As a HANDLER of these important processes...were you not aware of this from the very beginning or were you simply just nodding along para lang matapos yung trabaho mo? [REDACTED_NAME] I am fully aware that big universities have a standard they must hold their students up to. However, by those standards, it is also fair that students expect a high level of professionalism and handling of cases like these. I am not upset about the rejection. I am not even upset about all of the expenses that reached over 2k (not including transpo, etc.) and time spent on gathering things like the necessary papers and effort spent walking from building to building just to fill out forms and collect said papers. I am upset that I was allowed to continue until the very end only to be told the news, with the lack of professionalism as I was constantly spoken to in a condescending tone and backhanded comments, with me being allowed to proceed to only be met with "sorry" after everything. This experience has been one of the absolute worst I have had in terms of handling. So with that being said, to incoming freshmen or transferees, ALWAYS ask questions. Kahit paulitulit pa yan. Keep. Asking. Questions. So as to not waste your time like I did mine at para alam mo din kung all good ka ba or may [REDACTED_NAME] iprepare. [REDACTED_NAME] ipa confirm kung tama ba [REDACTED_NAME] [REDACTED_NAME] staff or hindi. To the Louisians, stay strong! Keep fighting! And [REDACTED_NAME]. 💕

I miss myself, the way I was, When the clouds went away. I was radiant. I was happy. I was sure of myself. I was quick-witted, I was talented I was full of promise. I was well-liked, I was gliding through it I wasn't drowning, I was riding out the waves There were simply more reasons to like myself. I don't know why I became this way. I thought love would heal me, patch me up But I became you, became someone I don't even want to look at. I ruin everything now. I carried my pain through every new face, Through every new person who tried. Eventually, they would just become familiar. I look down now when I walk in hallways. I don't want to see their indifferent faces That were once full of joy to see me. I miss the different versions of myself that lived before and died. If they were here in my stead I wouldn't have to cry myself to sleep then.

Healing from the things we don't deserve is hard, even if we have already accepted them and continue to move on with our lives. Although I've been doing fine these past few months, all the memories suddenly reappear. This haunts me, and I feel so down because no matter how much I assure myself that it's okay to let it out and feel everything, no matter how much I tell myself that it's okay now, that it's done, you're free, you learned, it feels like everything isn't enough. I'm tired of overthinking, weary of letting go, and tired of these feelings. Regardless, I deserved all the love regardless of what I had experienced. I hope we can all heal from the things that traumatized us and from the things we can't tell. I really hope that one day I can wake up and be free like it's really nothing.

Additional comment to the “Shouldn’t a Teacher... Teach?” post because I forgot to mention this: One time, during class, one of my classmates broke down in tears while the instructor was, yet again, just reading from her paper instead of actually engaging us in discussion as we took notes as quickly as possible. My classmate cried because of her distress to what was happening in class ([REDACTED_NAME] take note [REDACTED_NAME] and [REDACTED_NAME]). After a few minutes, she tried to return to the room. But as soon as the teacher saw her through the glass window thingy on the door, she stood up and locked it. Medyo natakot [REDACTED_NAME]to, actually. Then she said, “Hindi nagpaalam eh.” Two of my classmate’s friends told the instructor that she had a breakdown. So, of course, everyone can assume that that means she wasn’t in the right state of mind to ask for permission earlier. Despite that, the instructor stayed silent. That kind of silent anger, especially from someone in a position of authority, can be as intimidating as yelling. [REDACTED_NAME] [REDACTED_NAME] silence niya. And to the girl who commented on the original post saying: “Instructors" - instruct, "teachers" - teach, sorry to say this but it is the reality ever since. Advice as an ate alumni, let's strive harder on our own and ask our instructor more. Let's give them the benefit of the doubt. I appreciate your intention to give advice. But here’s the thing: we already strive harder than most people think. We study, we do our own research, we help each other out, we ask questions. Just because this kind of system has been the “reality ever since” doesn’t mean we should continue to accept it in silence. A broken reality doesn’t get better by brushing it off, it improves when people speak up and demand change, even in merely little ways. So no, we’re not complaining just because we want to.

Surround yourselves with people who will stand with you, and will support you throughout your journey. ❗️❗️❗️❗️❗️ REMINDER to all the girls: GIRL CODE is more than just a rule, it’s a quiet understanding. A kind of respect that doesn’t always need to be explained. It’s about choosing your friend’s heart over temporary attention, and knowing when something isn’t worth risking a friendship for. I never thought I’d have to question the loyalty of someone I trusted. I believed that what I felt was understood, that it didn’t need to be spelled out, that real friends just know, and act accordingly. But lately, I’ve learned that not everyone values things the same way. What hurts the most isn’t just what happened, it’s the silence. The fact that it was kept from me. That she let me find out on my own. That she chose not to say anything at all. It didn’t need to be something serious for it to matter. The fact that you knew how your friend felt should’ve been enough to remind you where to stand. Friends know when to draw the line, and real ones respect it. Friendship should be a place of safety, where you don’t have to constantly explain how to be respected. Where your feelings are protected, not ignored. So to every girl reading this: know the code. Honor the people who trust you. And when faced with a choice, choose your friend.

You ever get that feeling that you don’t belong in your own “friend” group? That’s me right now. It’s a different kind of loneliness, y’know? The feeling of being expendable, of being someone who will not be missed. I always liked to believe that I was good at making friends. Maybe at some point I was. Well, not anymore. Not after everything that happened. I got myself stuck in this purgatory inside a friend group that I don’t really feel any close connection to. A friend group that is very responsible, very organized and are composed of overall good people. In terms of academics and university life, I would say they’re a really good influence. But when push comes to shove, there are qualities in many members that I am not comfortable with. I don’t like the constant mood swings. The constant negative hatred towards other people. The pressure of keeping up academically. It’s suffocating. That’s not even the worst part. I am always the last one to be considered for anything we do. An afterthought, a ghost and a simple add-on. I am left out on many topics, not approached for anything, and not even someone relevant enough to be part of the conversation. Sure, I throw my jokes, my quips and my charms to the table. But sometimes, I get tired of trying to impress people who don’t really see the value in me as a genuine friend. Just someone who’s valuable to be around with. Or at least thats how I feel. Thats how their actions made me feel. “Why won’t you just leave”, you might think. It’s university. You have to be realistic about these things. I hold no ill will towards the friend group personally. I doubt they realize what I feel. I doubt they’d care much. Semester is over, and I am glad to be done with pretending. Just a few more years and I can finally ghost. Start a new thing someplace else, and get to know other people that will care about me. Till then, I have my anonymous freedom wall rants to keep me afloat. Have a great vacation everyone.

Hi gwani 🧸, I don’t know if you’ll ever read something like this. I’m not even sure I’d ever want to send it. But I think I just need to say it — not for you, but for me. That day we saw each other — it felt like stepping into a version of time that had paused and waited. But we weren’t the same. We were different. Older. Softer in some ways, more distant in others. And yet, there were things in that moment that made me feel like we hadn’t really let go — not completely. You initiated the conversations. You told stories. You remembered things that, honestly, I didn’t expect you to still carry — like that number. The one only you knew. The one you didn’t even need to check twice because you already had it memorized. That moment hit me hard. Not because I forgot about the number, but because I realized... you hadn’t. And I saw it in your silence, in your hands, in your eyes avoiding mine. You were carrying something too. Maybe confusion, maybe old feelings, maybe just the ache of remembering that there was a time when we were everything to each other. When I reflexively reached out to pull you out of the way of that tricycle, I didn’t think. My body just moved. Because even after all this time, some part of me still reacts to protect you. You shrugged me off — and I get it now. It wasn’t rejection. It was distance. Maybe protection. Maybe a boundary you needed to keep between us. But that touch... even for a second, it reminded me that I’m still wired to care. And maybe that’s okay, even if nothing else comes of it. I don’t hate you. I never did. And I don’t regret what we were. Even if it ended. Even if it got messy. Even if it still hurts sometimes when I least expect it to. Maybe in another version of our story, we’d meet again under different circumstances. One where we could look at each other without the weight of what we’ve lost. But this time? It felt like goodbye, even if neither of us said it. So here’s me saying it now: Thank you for showing up that day. Thank you for being honest in the little ways you could. And thank you — even in silence — for still holding on to parts of me for so long. But I’m letting go now. Not because I want to forget you. But because I finally can. — P P.S. A girl from [NCR], a soul from [CAR] — maybe we were always meant to meet halfway, but never stay.

Please be mindful of your surroundings, whether you are in the hallways or the canteen. I understand it is not a library, but since the usual shortcuts to the library are currently blocked due to construction, getting there has become more difficult. As a result, many students have started reviewing in common areas. That said, I have noticed that some groups tend to be very loud, sometimes to the point of being disruptive. The frequent use of curse words also creates an uncomfortable environment for others. I am not sure when it became acceptable to curse so openly on campus, but it is important to remember that school is still a place where respect and proper behavior should be observed. This is not about pretending to be someone you are not. It is simply about showing decency and consideration for others. We are already in college and should know how to carry ourselves properly. You can enjoy conversations with your friends without shouting.

Shouldn’t a Teacher... Teach? We’re Tired of the “Spoon-Fed” Excuse. We Deserve Real Educators. Hi, just wanted to know what are your thoughts on this. I hope you guys make time to read this. For our *** *** class, I had high hopes. On the first day of the second semester, during our course orientation, I found the instructor funny and cool. I was genuinely excited to learn more about the subject because it felt incredibly relevant to my program and personal interests. But now that the semester is ending, I can confidently and honestly say that not once did she actually teach or facilitate a meaningful discussion in class. I’m not even exaggerating. At one point, she even mentioned having a heavy workload, and said it as if to gain sympathy. But still, no change. Her typical routine consists of flashing a PowerPoint presentation, staying completely silent for 30 seconds to 2 minutes so we can copy the content, then moving on to the next slide. If there’s no presentation, she reads from her notes and expects us to write everything down at lightning speed. Every few seconds, students plead, “Ma’am, ang bilis po!” or “Ma’am, paulit po pleasee”, but she never acknowledges them. Not even once. She said we should learn how to properly take notes. At one point, before prelims began, she asked students she had already handled to leave the room so she could speak privately to the new ones. She asked what we thought about her teaching. One of my classmates respectfully shared that she learns better through discussions than by copying dictated notes. Did anything change after that? Sadly, no. Instead, she continued to randomly call on students to explain terms and concepts, without ever explaining them herself. Like?! Isn’t that the role of an instructor? What makes it worse is that many students, especially those she previously handled, have already complained. Her only justification was that we shouldn’t be “spoon-fed” and should learn on our own. But isn’t that the very essence of education? Instruction comes first. Then students internalize, reflect, and apply. Self-directed learning should be encouraged, but never at the expense of actual teaching. I had no idea whether anyone formally reported her. I would have done so myself if I knew that the process to hold instructors accountable, or at least push for improvement, was simple and effective. It seems incredibly difficult to initiate any real action. That realization was disheartening. It left me feeling powerless and frustrated, knowing that many students were being deprived of the education they deserve. When midterm grades were released, nearly half the class failed. And for finals? All we did was submit a video project. Those who failed midterms were asked to “recover” by doing classroom presentations. So for several days, we sat through the same topics being presented over and over and over and over again. Topics that the instructor should have taught in the first place. It felt like the entire class was doing her job. Don’t even get me started on her exams. It completely misaligned with the nature of the subject. Instead of thoughtful, reflective questions that encourage critical thinking, she gives fill-in-the-blank tests, expecting students to memorize entire paragraphs word-for-word, like it’s a math exam with only one correct answer. It’s like asking, “What’s a synonym for ‘good’?” and she only accepts “nice” while rejecting equally valid answers like “excellent,” “splendid,” or “satisfactory.” This course is supposed to celebrate diverse thinking and perspectives, yet her teaching style does the opposite. And to make matters worse, it’s clear she plays favorites. Some students are treated more kindly, while others are dismissed or ignored. My enthusiasm for this subject, which I once looked forward to, has been completely diminished. Learning should be engaging, eye-opening, and empowering. Instead, we were left to teach ourselves while she stood back and watched. We’re Not Asking for Much

Hello [REDACTED_NAME], I just want to ask for help and maybe share a little bit of what I've been going through. Recently, I was scammed. I know it might sound foolish to some, but as a student who can't rely on my parents for support, I worked really hard and saved up every peso to buy an iPad for school. Carrying around my reviewers and printing notes daily has been difficult, and I thought this device could really help me manage my studies better. I came across a secondhand iPad on Facebook Marketplace. I wasn't planning to buy one at first, since it was out of my budget, but this deal seemed reasonable. I did everything I could to verify the seller; checked her ID, the rider’s plate number, and trusted her word. Sadly, after sending the money, she disappeared. The device never arrived. What hurts the most is the sacrifices I made to save that money: skipping meals, walking long distances instead of taking another jeep, and choosing not to spend on basic comforts just so I could afford something that would help me in school. Now it’s all gone, and I'm left with anger, sadness, and so much disappointment. Every day, I try to smile and pretend everything’s okay, but deep down I feel like a fool. That’s why I’m reaching out. If anyone knows of any part-time jobs, side hustles, or online opportunities I could do as a student, I’d truly appreciate the help. Even a prayer means a lot to me right now. Thank you so much for reading this.

Hi everyone. This is actually my first time doing something like this—sending a confession to an FB group. I've been going back and forth about whether I should share this or not. It's something that's been on my mind for years—yes, literal years. I’ve just always felt so conflicted. Taking up Architecture was a big gamble for me. Not just because it’s a difficult course with no guaranteed income after graduation, but because of my personal circumstances. I’m the first in our small family to reach college. I’m also the panganay, and I had no one to guide me on how college life works—especially here at [CAR]. Everything I have now is from pure grit and stubborn effort. I didn’t come from a family with a firm waiting for me. I didn’t grow up surrounded by titos or cousins who are architects who could help when I was struggling to understand something. I entered this course with almost nothing—barely any materials—until I decided to work graveyard from Mondays through Sundays as a freelancer to be able to afford them. My parents cover my tuition, and I’m deeply thankful, but I know it’s a little heavy on them too. There were even times when some professors (yes, including that one kalbo na prof in Arki) told me maybe Architecture just wasn’t for me. And coming from someone in the field, it was a honestly a little upsetting. But I’d like to believe my mental fortitude is strong—so I shrugged it off. [REDACTED_NAME] bashers. I’ve always known this path would be hard. But what made it even harder was the alienating air in the [DEPARTMENT]. As an irregular student, I often felt like I didn’t belong. There’s a subtle—sometimes not-so-subtle—divide between regular and irregular students. I’ve been intentionally excluded or overlooked during group projects, especially when grouped with block sections. I’ve clashed with a few students who misread my silence in group discussions as being uncooperative, when in fact, I already completed my share of work even before she asked for it. It became clear to me that there’s little to no sense of community in the Architecture department at [CAR]. And when both professors and peers make you feel like you were wrong to pursue this path, you begin to question yourself talaga. I realized—maybe it’s not Architecture I need to give up on...�Maybe it’s [CAR]. It’s bittersweet. I’ve had fun. I’ve made good friends and I’ve met amazing people. I won’t, can’t, and don’t want to give up on my dream of becoming a licensed architect someday. Because this dream isn’t just mine—�I’m carrying my family’s dream too. Especially my dad’s. He never had the chance to go to college, but deep down, he admitted he would’ve wanted to become an architect himself. So I’m walking this road not just for me, but for him and for all the sacrifices my family has made. It’s a little heavy on the heart, but I know this choice is leading me somewhere better. Somewhere where I can truly grow, belong, and keep chasing this dream. God is faithful, always. To all irregular arki students like me out there, or irregular students in general, I read a great quote somewhere that goes like this, “What a privilege it is to be tired in the pursuit of a challenge of your own choosing” Mapapagod pero ‘di susuko. ’Til we meet again, [CAR].

To the person who posted about the IE student on the freedom wall, what you did was not only inappropriate, it was disrespectful. It is not your place to share someone else’s personal struggles, especially not in such a public manner. The details surrounding his passing are not yours to disclose. Dropping his name and speculating about the reasons behind what happened was a violation of his privacy and, more importantly, a violation of the grief that his family and friends are still trying to process. If you knew him well, you would know how much he values his privacy. Family and friends are in shock and mourning. And the last thing we need is for his name to be turned into a discussion topic by people who did not even know him well. There is a difference between sympathizing and sensationalizing someone’s tragedy—and what you did crossed that line. [CAR] is a competitive institution, yes. But his passing is not something for everyone to know. To those who truly knew him, he was a person of deep faith, strength, and kindness. As his family would say, his mission on Earth has just finished. Let us honor him by offering prayers and respecting his privacy.

[Storytime: The Weight of Two Lines] We became a couple. Every time we met, something happened between us. At first, we were careful. We used protection. There was fear, there were questions. But as time went by, he became too comfortable. And I stayed quiet. Then one day, no more protection. No more fear. Every time he came inside me, he whispered promises sweeter than safety. Then one day... my period didn’t come. Time froze. The world blurred. There I was, sitting on cold tiles, holding a dream that turned into a ghost. Two lines. Two strokes. Two words in my mind: "It’s over." I told him— not for pity, but hoping he’d hold my hand in the storm we created. But what he said... cut deeper than silence. It felt like a knife wrapped in calm. No guilt. No pause. Just cold words: "That’s not my problem." "I can’t handle that." "I have a future. I’m a Dean Lister. I have dreams." He said it like I was nothing. Like I didn’t matter. Like the life growing inside me was a burden he had no time for. In that moment, I wasn’t his girlfriend. I wasn’t someone he loved. I was just a mistake he wanted to erase. Since that day, I’ve felt lost. I can’t eat. I can’t sleep. My body is tired, but my heart is even more tired. And every night, I ask myself: Does he still think of me? I saw him once. [Seminary] He was laughing with his friends, eating like nothing happened. While I stood there, holding coins in my hand and tears in my eyes. They looked at me— but no one asked if I was okay. No one cared. Now, I don’t know if I still need to study at [CAR]. I don’t know if I still want to live or stay. Sometimes, I just want to disappear— to leave my friends, my family... even myself. But deep inside, a small voice tells me: “This is not the end. You still matter. You are not the shame he left behind. You are a mother now. You are strong.” And if you ever hear this, not as your secret, not as your regret, but as the girl who loved you completely, and now carries the life you walked away from... I hope you remember: You left something behind. Not just a 👶🏻. But a heart still learning how to heal from the silence you gave me.

It frustrates me how so many young Filipinos, especially [REDACTED_NAME], have this mindset of “I’m not going to vote because no one deserves it” or “I won’t vote because the corrupt always win anyway.” Like seriously? Some don’t even bother to register because they’ve already given up on the system. Meron pa yung “di ako mag vovote [REDACTED_NAME] directly apektado” Oh my, how selfish are you?? [REDACTED_NAME] nasa laylayan? This mindset is exactly why nothing changes. If we keep turning our backs on our right to vote, we’re handing power over to the very people we complain about. And if we all keep thinking like that, then who’s left to actually change the system? Voting is not just a right it’s our responsibility. It’s one of the few powers we actually have as citizens. And the truth is, there will never be a perfect candidate. No one is perfect. All of us, whether politicians or ordinary people, have our own flaws. But that doesn’t mean we should just give up. There are people out there who are genuinely trying to make a difference, you just have to do the research. We are a product of many mistakes, generations and generations of them, but that doesn’t mean we’re doomed to repeat them. We can learn. We should learn. Maybe that’s the issue: people say no one is deserving, but they don’t even take the time to find out who’s out there. With all the access we have to information, that’s just plain laziness. Our generation is literally more educated than the ones before us. We should know better than to vote for someone just because they’re famous or the only name we recognize. So please, stop romanticizing not voting like it’s some kind of rebellion. It’s not. It’s ignorance. It’s just giving up. And we can’t afford to give up. Not now. Not ever. Wake up, youth. Wake up, Philippines.

When you join mobilizations without even understanding the ideological or policy platforms you supposedly support, you are not participating—you are reducing activism into performative activism. And when those expected to uphold political literacy—Comelec officers, POSAS officers, more so, political science students—parade political events as mere props for recitations and Instagram stories, it stops being praxis. It becomes pure posturing, and worse, a mockery of what true civic engagement demands. You are in a discipline that demands critical political engagement—not performative, not mimicry. To participate meaningfully is to wrestle with contradictions, to fight between conflicts and to provide synthesis in between. Kung tunay ang layunin, dapat [REDACTED_NAME]. This is just a call to stop reducing grandiose acts to your performative actions. Stop prostituting activism for academic clout and personal branding. Do better, officers. Let your actions speak of principle, not of empty validation and your clout chasing principles. Tigilan niyo ang pagsakay sa mga kampanyang hindi niyo man [REDACTED_NAME]. The [CAR] KASAMA/SSC stands in solidarity with the studentry in the fight against harassment in all its forms. We are committed to fostering safe spaces for everyone and actively working towards social justice and harmony within our community. However, for the past few months, we are deeply concerned by the recent allegations of sexual harassment involving recognized students and even student officers within our university. Thus, consistent with the principles and mandates of the Safe Spaces Act (Republic Act ), the Code of Conduct under the Student Handbook of [CAR], and the KASAMA/SSC Constitution of and By-Laws of , the council firmly believes that every student has the right to a safe and nurturing environment, free from fear and intimidation, where they can pursue their academic and personal growth. Additionally, we stand firm that harassment of any kind is unacceptable and goes against the core values which [CAR] upholds. We will not tolerate such behavior and will actively support efforts to address these issues, ensuring that the voices of those affected are heard and that appropriate action is taken, in accordance with the provisions of the Safe Spaces Act and the Student Handbook. The council is dedicated to collaborating with the university administration, faculty, and student body to strengthen mechanisms for reporting harassment, providing support for victims, and implementing preventative measures. With this, in creating an effective avenue for grievance and suggestions, we are extending our helping hand to everyone who can give details and can actively participate in the investigation of these cases. You can submit your concern through the [REDACTED_DEMONYM] Grievance Box: directly message the [CAR] KASAMA/SSC Page, and/or submit an incident report directly to [CAR] Office of Student Affairs and Services. We urge the student body to utilize the provided avenues to report incidents and share information, as this can be crucial in ensuring a thorough and just resolution to these cases. Together, let us work towards building a [REDACTED_DEMONYM] community where respect, safety, and justice prevail. [REDACTED_NAME] [REDACTED_NAME], dahil Luwisyano, .

Hi! I’m a 222 MLS student :> and I have found genuine friendships with the 223 MLS batch. This batch truly is the kindest and most innocent group. As an irregular student, I felt comfortable studying with them because most of them don’t judge you. Instead, they ask questions so they can learn as well. Even though I failed before, I felt okay sharing with them what mistakes I made that caused me to fail the subject. This way, they won’t make the same errors and have to retake it like I did. The 223 batch is more than just blockmates to me. When they see me stressed about not graduating on time, they remind me it’s okay to take my time. Because of them, I realized that being behind is not the same as failing. Life isn’t about rushing to the end, but about growing and learning along the way. The ultimate goal is to become [REDACTED_NAME], ready and proud. They’re really happy to have met me, and even if I failed before, they appreciate that I’m helping them avoid those same mistakes. - From a very pressured 222 MLS student

ELEVATOR EXPERIENCE Calling the attention of maintenance at [CAR] in the Perfecto Building. I often ride the elevator because my room is on the 7th floor. This isn't the first time I've had an incident like this in the elevator. My first encounter was on the first day of the second semester, when I was with my former classmates from the first semester. We got stuck for a few minutes, but I wasn’t too affected. However, today at around 11:00 AM, none of us expected to go through something like this. There were nine of us in the elevator which is under its 15-person capacity. we were even laughing and joking around, when suddenly the elevator dropped from the 4th floor to the 2nd floor as we were heading down to the 1st. Some of my classmates panicked including me. We didn't expect that. Their response was agonizingly slow. We hit the open button but nothing happened. Luckily, by the time we hit the emergency button and reached the 2nd floor, someone noticed us. A group of people rushed over and worked together trying to open the doors since the open button still wouldn’t register. They even had to break the door. It was incredibly traumatizing, especially because of how long it took for anyone to help. It was a life or death situation. Thankfully, no one was hurt, but the impact to me was so severe that I still can't get over from time to time. I just hope this never happens to anyone else. - 1st year psych student

My (22F) boyfriend (23M) is an S-brand phone user and I used to be one (now I'm just an Android girlie). I know some secret hacks and stuff about S-brand and I shared all of them with him since he became an S-brand user, one of which is the "Secure Folder" feature of S-brand. I have full access to his phone whenever we meet, and last week (Holy Week, talk about the irony), while he's taking a bath, I used his phone since mine's low batt. I felt an urge to check his gallery in the SF expecting nothing from it and failed miserably. I found couple of videos of a specific couple (like, 'tong couple [REDACTED_NAME]to 'yung nasa SF gallery) doing the deed. At first, I was taken aback because WHY DOES HE HAVE SCANDALS ON HIS PHONE? We talked about this kind of stuff before and we're both against people sharing and downloading intimate videos without the consent of those involved in the video, so I was really baffled when I saw those. My second taught was MAYBE they're his friends and he got it from them directly, because I've heard stories of L-n students being wild af that sometimes they openly share intimate videos of them with their s/o or hook-up to their friends (nonetheless, I still think it's very inappropriate to watch it, even saving it). But as I scrolled through the SF, I got more and more confused, nervous, jealous, upset, lahat na ng negative, because I saw pictures (selfies, thirst traps, nudes) of the girl ('yung girl do'n sa videos). To save their scandal is one thing, but to find out na he also saved non-intimate ones like selfies is another. Parang nagka-tinnitus ako that time, my mind went blank and my sight went blurry. I couldn't move for a while because I was having a hard time making sense out of the things I saw. Seeing the videos had me thinking na maybe he's just THAT horny, but it's the selfies that I can't explain; they looked "saved" and not taken with his camera since the photos are bit grainy (constant). My thoughts were: 1. Is he cheating on me wih the girl? If yes, then why is there only 1 guy, that's not him, in the videos? 2. Does he know them personally? Who could they possibly be? 3. Is he trying to hide it from me? Why does he have those in his SF gallery when he knows damn well that I'm the one who taught him the feature? (if he's hiding it from me, then that's just dumb) My boyfriend's very physical. And I hate having to admit this. Vote for [REDACTED_NAME], Vote for Bam. But they are really the only opposition candidates who have a real shot at winning. Vote for [REDACTED_NAME], Arambulo, etc? You basically assure the victory of more PDP-Laban candidates. One would argue that staying true to their chosen candidates is important as you want and need to make a statement. But is it truly worth it if the candidate loses and has no impact to the government afterward? What is that statement going to do when Philippine politics in status quo revolves around [REDACTED_NAME], and the distant future is dependent on this Midterm election? Honestly, May 12 is going to be all about strategy and having a sense of urgency. If PDP-Laban wins, and we fail to impeach [REDACTED_NAME]. You just know that when she runs in and has a huge chance in winning, The Dutertes will have the Philippines under control for good. Don't let that happen. Be smart about your votes. The opposition will live to fight another day when the Dutertes are out of the question. I hate the idea of having to vote for some [REDACTED_NAME] candidates, but if it means blocking more Pro-Duterte candidates from sitting in the senate, then I will have to do whats necessary to make that happen. I've hated the [REDACTED_NAME] and Dutertes ever since. But right now, this is all about choosing the lesser evil. It is also how "The enemy of my enemy is my friend". This election will dictate the future of the Philippines for decades to come. May the God's above us and the Universe have mercy on our beloved country. P.S: No to political dynasty in Baguio City!

HELP STRAYS IN BAGUIO-BENGUET! Furvent Animal Rescue and Advocacy, a Baguio-based rescue group, is in dire need of your help. Recently, one of their furbabies died from chronic disease complications including canine distemper virus. A lot of their dogs were exposed to the virus and are now in need of additional protection. Most of them include senior dogs, dogs with heart problems, and dogs that are on long-term medications. They also have dogs that are currently confined and still recovering due to chronic diseases as well as injuries from animal abuse. Unfortunately, to make things worse, one of their helpers ran away and took some of the donations for the rescued dogs. I know this page gains traction mostly from students that may also be struggling financially, but spreading the word already means a lot! Please like the Facebook page and engage with their posts, so it could reach those with means to help. You may also donate or volunteer if you can. Any amount helps! Thank you, Louisians! 💙💛 Link to their page: (Furvent Animal Rescue and Advocacy)

To Sir B You're honestly my favorite teacher. You're so good at teaching and super inspiring, every time you remind us how important it is to study, it really motivates me. But sir... ever since finals season started, I’ve been feeling kinda scared of you already, huhu. Maybe I got a bit of PTSD from that surprise quiz during finals. Knowing that I really need to pull my grades up just to secure my short term, I’ve started to feel unsure... like what if I don’t pass? I don’t enjoy studying as much anymore, because now it's fear that’s pushing me — not motivation. I keep thinking there might be another surprise quiz. I mean, yes, it’s good that I’ve started doing more advanced reading, but I don’t enjoy learning Chem the way I used to. The pressure feels too heavy now. I passed both prelims and midterms, but it still feels like it’s not enough, especially with how intense finals are. Please sir, I hope you go back to how you used to be. I want to learn not because I’m scared, but because you inspire us, like you always do. You’re still the best Chem teacher ever. -pagod na 1st year MLS student

To the students in the hallways, especially during dismissal times Pls po wag kayo mag kwentuhan na nakaharang sa gitna ng hallway po!! The hallway is already cramped enough with students waiting on both sides to enter the classrooms and stalling in the middle of the hallway will only make it more congested. Apart from that, pls pick up the pace kung paalis po kayo sa classrooms since may mga students and instructors na may klase pa sa room ninyo. You can just step aside in the hallway to talk to each other or just go to the floor lobby or more open areas so that the hallways will not be congested. We may all feel that we are all main characters to walk slowly in the middle of the hallway or even the middle of the stairs but have some consideration for those who have classes after you and those about to be late po. It’s a feeling as comforting and familiar as the scent of pine needles after a rain shower in the mountains. Sometimes, I find myself simply observing you from a distance, at the hallway of perfecto, elevator, and sometimes in the main gate, noticing the way you interact with your friends and blockmates, it reminds me of the simple beauty and warmth of community life. I know this letter might feel unexpected, perhaps even a little overwhelming. And I completely understand if you don't reciprocate my feelings. But I felt compelled to share this with you, to let you know how much I appreciate you, not just for your outward charm, but for the beautiful person you are. This might seem odd to some, and I understand there will be people who’ll ask [REDACTED_NAME] ipost? [REDACTED_NAME] i-private message [REDACTED_NAME]? I get that. But this isn’t about humiliating anyone, especially that we are both in SEA. To the SEA freshie, a first-year male student whom I’ve never met personally and never had any real interaction with on campus, this message is for you. I, being your senior, have been hearing from others that you've been openly saying we’re together. You've been calling me your boyfriend, talking as if we’re in a relationship, when the truth is, we are not. We never had a real conversation. We don’t chat, we don’t hang out, and we never had any agreement or moment that could even be mistaken for something romantic. I want to start by saying this clearly. I’m not angry that you may have liked me. It’s not a crime to admire someone, to feel something, or even to wish for a connection. We are human. We feel. And I don’t want to make you feel ashamed of that. But what I cannot stay silent about is how you handled those feelings. Sharing things that aren’t true, telling people I’m your boyfriend, and building a story that involves me without my consent, those are not okay. It’s one thing to have feelings, but it’s another to involve someone else in your personal narrative without their knowledge or permission. Because of what you’ve said, someone very important to me was affected. I’ve been courting this girl for years. I’ve been consistent, sincere, and hopeful that someday she will see how genuine I’ve been. But when she heard that I was already taken, but when she believed the story that you’ve been sharing, she gave me a bitter no. Not because I did anything wrong, but because she believed a lie. A lie I had no part in creating. Do you know how that feels? To lose someone you’ve been holding onto for so long, because of something you didn’t even do? I can’t explain the level of pain and frustration that came with that. And that’s why I needed to speak up. This is something that broke a connection I’ve spent years nurturing. Again, I want to make it clear. I’m not here to shame you. I’m not here to mock your feelings. I understand what it’s like to admire someone from afar, to hope for something more. But I hope you also understand that there are proper ways to deal with those feelings. Creating false stories, telling people we are together, and allowing those lies to spread, those choices hurt people. They hurt me. They hurt her. And maybe, if you take a moment to reflect, they hurt you too. I’m not asking for an apology. I’m not expecting anything. But I am hoping that this helps you realize the importance of truth, boundaries, and respect. We can’t control who we like, but we can control how we act on those feelings. l So please, stop telling people we’re in a relationship. Stop calling me your boyfriend. And if there’s anything you need to work through, I hope you find healthier, kinder ways to do that. Not just for me, but for yourself and the people around you. I know that your feelings are valid, but they don’t give us the right to write stories in other people’s names.

Lowkey, I've always been on edge. Clinging on to any last drop of hope and happiness I have in my life. I am now devoid of the man I used to be. All I see now was my greatest fear of becoming. Wanting to change however certainty lingers that it won't do much. At the same time, uncertainty glides with the thought of, "What if it really does?". All I have is myself, however, that's the company I least wanted. I sink slowly. I am confused. I do not know where I am. I want to scream for help however pride gets in the way. I yearn but I don't want people to get uncomfortable. I've always been told, "You shouldn't care about what people think". From my stance, how can I not care when my smallest of actions can affect someone more than it does to me? I was always made to choose between people. A single person between a crowd. A crowd between a crowd. A person between a person. But as soon as I chose myself, everything went to shit. Why couldn't you just let me choose myself? I can feel the sensation of me eating away at myself. Gradually losing my thoughts --- my identity. I can no longer decide on what I want. I am lost. This changing made me lose who I was. Fuhk [REDACTED_NAME].

Grief is like a houseguest you can't bring yourself to stop inviting over. “It's cold outside; just spend the night.” “It's much too late; just stay.” “Don't worry, the couch is free.” So it stays and makes itself comfortable on your couch until morning comes. The new day arrives, and you catch sight of them sleeping soundly on your couch. Your gut turns, and you feel bad sending them off with an empty stomach. So you heat up the pan, crack an egg or two, and make them a cup of coffee. They wake up, enjoy their breakfast, and prolong the conversations until your clock reads noon. Your gut turns at unease again. You can’t let them go without a meal. So you take the meat out of the freezer and let it thaw. A hearty meal for both of you to share. Soon the afternoon weighs heavy upon the both of you, and you fall asleep together on the couch. You wake up when the sun no longer colors in the sky, and once again, gnawing unease fills your gut. They wake up at the sound of your movements, and they say, “Oh, look how late it is already.” You nod, and you reply, “Just stay. It's too late, anyway. It's not safe for you to walk at night. Besides, it's cold, and my couch is warm and free. So, stay.”

There was a time when I had a quiet crush on a girl from [REDACTED_NAME]. I met her unexpectedly, right in front of the [CAR] cashier. We were both waiting in arm chair, and I took a chance, I started a conversation. She smiled, and we talked for a bit. Before we parted ways, I asked for her Facebook. She gave it, and in that small moment, something felt special. I remember lying, saying I was waiting for my calculator, just to have a reason to stay longer and be near her. She was kind, gentle, and incredibly pretty. I couldn’t help but feel drawn to her. There were moments after that when I wanted to give her flowers. I’d walk past places thinking, “Maybe I’ll see her today.” One day, I actually did. I rushed to buy flowers, hoping I’d have the chance to give them to her, maybe even say something more. But by the time I came back, she was already gone. It was such a small thing, but it left me feeling a little heartbroken. But it's okay, no bid deal. I'm happy for her right now 🤍 Ps: But I never got to treat her the way I wanted to :< I never got to show her how much I admired her, even from afar. Hi, Gorgeous! Na ginagawa nya lahat lahat [REDACTED_NAME] efforts just to save their relationship. Gets?) This makes me very sad fr. Many women can endure all the pain and heartbreak for a man. In those tough times, when nothing seems clear, a woman will still stay because she loves and believes in him. Despite all the red flags and disrespect, she hoped he would change for the betterment of their relationship. But for some men, when they experience those down phases in the relationship, how can they easily decide to let go of all of it in just one snap? All the connections they've built together, all the memories, all their future plans, all the promises? It's supposed to be a relationship that ends with marriage. Of course, having those rough patches when you're in a long-term relationship is naturally part of it. It's supposed to be part of being committed to one another, right? Whatever happens, you stick together if you are really committed to one another. But if it's that easy for you to let it all go, does it only mean you're not fully committed to the relationship?
